171	fn selection_marker(selected: Option<Square>, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape) -> View {
172	    let Some(sq) = selected else { return ().into_view() };
173	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
174	    for row in 0..rows {
175	        for col in 0..cols {
176	            if square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) == Some(sq) {
177	                let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
178	                return view! { <circle class="selection" cx=cx cy=cy r="28"/> }.into_view();
179	            }
180	        }
181	    }
182	    ().into_view()
183	}
184	
185	fn chain_lock_marker(locked: Option<Square>, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape) -> View {
186	    let Some(sq) = locked else { return ().into_view() };
187	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
188	    for row in 0..rows {
189	        for col in 0..cols {
190	            if square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) == Some(sq) {
191	                let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
192	                return view! { <circle class="chain-lock" cx=cx cy=cy r="30"/> }.into_view();
193	            }
194	        }
195	    }
196	    ().into_view()
197	}
198	
199	fn move_dots_view(
200	    view: &PlayerView,
201	    selected: Option<Square>,
202	    observer: Side,
203	    shape: BoardShape,
204	) -> View {
205	    let Some(from) = selected else { return ().into_view() };
206	    let targets = legal_targets(view, from);
207	    if targets.is_empty() {
208	        return ().into_view();
209	    }
210	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
211	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::new();
212	    for row in 0..rows {
213	        for col in 0..cols {
214	            let Some(sq) = square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) else { continue };
215	            if !targets.contains(&sq) {
216	                continue;
217	            }
218	            let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
219	            // Empty target → small dot. Capturable target (revealed
220	            // enemy piece OR face-down tile under DARK_CAPTURE) →
221	            // larger ring framing the piece, so the player sees at a
222	            // glance which enemies / hidden tiles can be taken — not
223	            // just where to slide to. Same data the TUI surfaces with
224	            // its green-highlighted attackable squares.
225	            let cell = view.cells[sq.0 as usize];
226	            let class = match cell {
227	                VisibleCell::Empty => "move-dot",
228	                _ => "move-target",
229	            };
230	            let r = if class == "move-dot" { "9" } else { "26" };
231	            out.push(view! { <circle class=class cx=cx cy=cy r=r/> }.into_view());
232	        }
233	    }
234	    out.into_view()
235	}
236	
237	fn hit_cells(
238	    rows: u8,
239	    cols: u8,
240	    observer: Side,
241	    shape: BoardShape,
242	    on_click: Callback<Square>,
243	) -> View {
244	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::with_capacity(rows as usize * cols as usize);
245	    for row in 0..rows {
246	        for col in 0..cols {
247	            let Some(sq) = square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) else { continue };
248	            let x = col as i32 * CELL;
249	            let y = row as i32 * CELL;
250	            out.push(
251	                view! {
252	                    <rect
253	                        class="cell-hit"
254	                        x=x y=y width=CELL height=CELL
255	                        on:pointerup=move |_| on_click.call(sq)
256	                    />
257	                }
258	                .into_view(),
259	            );
260	        }
261	    }
262	    out.into_view()
263	}

```text
1	//! Full-screen "VICTORY / DEFEAT / DRAW" celebration overlay.
2	//!
3	//! The overlay is gated by:
4	//!   1. the user's `fx_confetti` pref (default ON), and
5	//!   2. an Ongoing → Won/Drawn status transition.
6	//!
7	//! Spectators (and Local pass-and-play, which has no `role`) get neutral
8	//! "Red Wins / Black Wins / Draw" copy. Net players see the personalised
9	//! "VICTORY" / "DEFEAT" framing. The overlay auto-dismisses after ~3s
10	//! via `set_timeout`; the matching sidebar status text stays as a
11	//! permanent record of the result.
12	//!
13	//! The component owns a `prev_status` signal so a remount (e.g. lobby
14	//! navigation) doesn't re-fire on the persistent end state. New games
15	//! reset the tracker via the Ongoing → ended check.
16	
17	use std::time::Duration;
18	
19	use chess_core::piece::Side;
20	use chess_core::state::{DrawReason, GameStatus, WinReason};
21	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
22	use leptos::*;
23	
24	use crate::components::confetti::Confetti;
25	use crate::state::ClientRole;
26	
27	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
28	enum BannerKind {
29	    Victory,
30	    Defeat,
31	    Draw,
32	    NeutralWin(Side),
33	}
34	
35	#[component]
36	pub fn EndOverlay(
37	    /// Live `PlayerView` — drives the status transition watcher.
38	    #[prop(into)]
39	    view: Signal<PlayerView>,
40	    /// `None` for Local pass-and-play (uses neutral copy); `Some(role)` for Net.
41	    #[prop(into)]
42	    role: Signal<Option<ClientRole>>,
43	    /// User pref — when false the overlay never mounts.
44	    #[prop(into)]
45	    enabled: Signal<bool>,
46	) -> impl IntoView {
47	    let (visible, set_visible) = create_signal(false);
48	    let (kind_signal, set_kind) = create_signal::<Option<BannerKind>>(None);
49	    let prev_status = create_rw_signal::<Option<GameStatus>>(None);
50	
51	    create_effect(move |_| {
52	        let cur = view.with(|v| v.status);
53	        let prev = prev_status.get_untracked();
54	        let was_ongoing = matches!(prev, Some(GameStatus::Ongoing));
55	        let now_ended = matches!(cur, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. });
56	
57	        if was_ongoing && now_ended && enabled.get_untracked() {
58	            let resolved_kind = resolve_kind(&cur, role.get_untracked());
59	            set_kind.set(resolved_kind);
60	            set_visible.set(true);
61	            // Auto-dismiss after the confetti animation finishes. Use
62	            // `set_timeout` rather than CSS animation events so the
63	            // banner fade and the confetti fade share one source of truth.
64	            set_timeout(
65	                move || {
66	                    set_visible.set(false);
67	                },
68	                Duration::from_millis(3200),
69	            );
70	        }
71	        prev_status.set(Some(cur));
72	    });
73	
74	    let title = move || match kind_signal.get() {
75	        Some(BannerKind::Victory) => "VICTORY".to_string(),
76	        Some(BannerKind::Defeat) => "DEFEAT".to_string(),
77	        Some(BannerKind::Draw) => "DRAW".to_string(),
78	        Some(BannerKind::NeutralWin(Side::RED)) => "Red Wins 紅勝".to_string(),
79	        Some(BannerKind::NeutralWin(Side::BLACK)) => "Black Wins 黑勝".to_string(),
80	        Some(BannerKind::NeutralWin(_)) => "Green Wins 綠勝".to_string(),
```

```text
1	# Trunk dev-server config. The single `/ws` proxy entry covers BOTH
2	# `/ws` (default-room upgrade for v1 chess-tui clients) and `/ws/<room>`
3	# (named room) because Trunk nests the proxy as a prefix route in axum.
4	# Registering separate `/ws` and `/ws/` entries panics on startup with
5	# "conflict with previously registered route" — see the gotcha note in
6	# `docs/trunk-leptos-wasm.md`.
7	#
8	# `addresses` is the new (Trunk 0.21+) replacement for the deprecated
9	# `address` scalar. Production-mode prod is chess-net's `--static-dir`
10	# path; Trunk is dev-only.
11	
12	[build]
13	target  = "index.html"
14	release = false
15	
16	[serve]
17	addresses = ["127.0.0.1"]
18	port      = 8080
19	open      = false
20	
21	# WebSocket upgrades — chess-net's /ws and /ws/<room> routes.
22	[[proxy]]
23	backend = "ws://127.0.0.1:7878/ws"
24	ws      = true
25	rewrite = "/ws"
26	
27	# Lobby push channel.
28	[[proxy]]
29	backend = "ws://127.0.0.1:7878/lobby"
30	ws      = true
31	rewrite = "/lobby"
32	
33	# Snapshot endpoint (debug; the SPA itself uses the lobby ws).
34	[[proxy]]
35	backend = "http://127.0.0.1:7878/rooms"
36	rewrite = "/rooms"
37
```
</details></tool-use>

1	fn main() {
2	    println!("cargo:rerun-if-env-changed=CHESS_WEB_HOSTING");
3	    println!("cargo:rerun-if-env-changed=CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH");
4	
5	    let hosting = std::env::var("CHESS_WEB_HOSTING").unwrap_or_else(|_| "server".to_string());
6	    let hosting = match hosting.as_str() {
7	        "static" => "static",
8	        _ => "server",
9	    };
10	
11	    let raw_base = std::env::var("CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH").unwrap_or_default();
12	    let base = normalize_base_path(&raw_base);
13	
14	    println!("cargo:rustc-env=CHESS_WEB_HOSTING={hosting}");
15	    println!("cargo:rustc-env=CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH={base}");
16	}
17	
18	fn normalize_base_path(raw: &str) -> String {
19	    let trimmed = raw.trim().trim_matches('/');
20	    if trimmed.is_empty() {
21	        String::new()
22	    } else {
23	        format!("/{trimmed}")
24	    }
25	}

1	//! `chess-web` — Leptos + WASM frontend for chess-core.
2	//!
3	//! Native (`cargo check --workspace`) compiles only the pure-logic modules
4	//! (orient, glyph, routes, state); the leptos UI and the WS layer are
5	//! wasm32-gated so the workspace target check stays clean. Build for the
6	//! browser with `trunk serve` (or `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown
7	//! -p chess-web`).

.game-page {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) 280px;
    gap: 1.5rem;
    align-items: start;
}
.game-page--single { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
@media (max-width: 720px) { .game-page { grid-template-columns: 1fr; } }

/* Right-hand column on the play page stacks the sidebar and chat panel. */
.right-column {
    display: grid;
    gap: 1rem;
    align-content: start;
}

.chat-panel {
    background: rgba(244, 210, 163, 0.04);
    border: 1px solid var(--grid);
    border-radius: 0.5rem;
    display: grid;
    grid-template-rows: 1fr auto;
    height: 320px;
    overflow: hidden;
}
.chat-log {
    overflow-y: auto;
    padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem;

 * ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

/* Centered VICTORY/DEFEAT/DRAW banner over the board area. The container
 * is `position: absolute` so it lives inside `.board-pane`'s positioning
 * context (same as `.wip-overlay`). The banner card uses the existing
 * accent palette so it reads as part of the theme rather than a popover. */

    position: absolute;
    inset: 0;
    display: grid;
    place-items: center;
    z-index: 40;
    pointer-events: none;
    background: rgba(26, 22, 20, 0.45);
    border-radius: 0.5rem;

}

```text
1	//! WebSocket pump for chess-net protocol. One read task fans server frames
2	//! into a `ServerMsg` signal; one write task drains an mpsc of `ClientMsg`s
3	//! to the server.
4	//!
5	//! No reconnect in PR-1 — see `backlog/web-ws-reconnect.md` for the planned
6	//! exponential-backoff retry. On disconnect or error, the conn-state signal
7	//! flips to `Closed`/`Error` and the page surfaces a "reload" toast.
8	
9	use chess_net::protocol::{ClientMsg, ServerMsg};
10	use futures::channel::mpsc::{unbounded, UnboundedSender};
11	use futures::{SinkExt, StreamExt};
12	use gloo_net::websocket::{futures::WebSocket, Message};
13	use leptos::*;
14	
15	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
16	pub enum ConnState {
17	    Connecting,
18	    Open,
19	    Closed,
20	    Error,
21	}
22	
23	#[derive(Clone)]
24	pub struct WsHandle {
25	    sender: UnboundedSender<ClientMsg>,
26	}
27	
28	impl WsHandle {
29	    /// Send a `ClientMsg`. Returns false if the write pump has shut down.
30	    pub fn send(&self, msg: ClientMsg) -> bool {
31	        self.sender.unbounded_send(msg).is_ok()
32	    }
33	}
34	
35	/// Open a WS connection. Returns:
36	///   * a handle to send `ClientMsg`s,
37	///   * a signal that latches the most recent `ServerMsg`,
38	///   * a connection-state signal.
39	pub fn connect(url: String) -> (WsHandle, ReadSignal<Option<ServerMsg>>, ReadSignal<ConnState>) {
40	    let (incoming, set_incoming) = create_signal::<Option<ServerMsg>>(None);
41	    let (state, set_state) = create_signal(ConnState::Connecting);
42	    let (out_tx, mut out_rx) = unbounded::<ClientMsg>();
43	
44	    match WebSocket::open(&url) {
45	        Ok(ws) => {
46	            let (mut tx, mut rx) = ws.split();
47	            set_state.set(ConnState::Open);
48	
49	            // Read pump — server frames → signal.
50	            wasm_bindgen_futures::spawn_local(async move {
51	                while let Some(msg) = rx.next().await {
52	                    match msg {
53	                        Ok(Message::Text(t)) => {
54	                            if let Ok(m) = serde_json::from_str::<ServerMsg>(&t) {
55	                                set_incoming.set(Some(m));
56	                            }
57	                        }
58	                        Ok(Message::Bytes(_)) => {}
59	                        Err(_) => {
60	                            set_state.set(ConnState::Error);
61	                            return;
62	                        }
63	                    }
64	                }
65	                set_state.set(ConnState::Closed);
66	            });
67	
68	            // Write pump — drain mpsc → server.
69	            wasm_bindgen_futures::spawn_local(async move {
70	                while let Some(cm) = out_rx.next().await {
71	                    let body = match serde_json::to_string(&cm) {
72	                        Ok(b) => b,
73	                        Err(_) => continue,
74	                    };
75	                    if tx.send(Message::Text(body)).await.is_err() {
76	                        break;
77	                    }
78	                }
79	            });
80	        }
81	        Err(_) => {
82	            set_state.set(ConnState::Error);
83	        }
84	    }
85	
86	    (WsHandle { sender: out_tx }, incoming, state)
87	}
88
```
</details></tool-use>

## Report: VS AI Mode Integration Plan - Chess-Web Crate Inventory

### 1. **Routing (`/local/:variant` URL parameter structure)**

**File**: `/Users/david/Documents/Program/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs` (lines 1-227)

**Current setup**:
- `parse_variant_slug()` (line 75) — parses URL slug: `"xiangqi" | "banqi" | "three-kingdom"`
- `LocalRulesParams` struct (lines 95-105) — holds parsed query state:
  - `strict: bool` — Xiangqi only, self-check filter
  - `house: HouseRules` — Banqi bitflags (chain, dark, rush, horse, cannon)
  - `seed: Option<u64>` — Banqi shuffle seed
- `parse_local_rules()` (line 113) — parses query string: `?strict=1&house=chain,rush&preset=taiwan&seed=42`
- `build_local_query()` (line 125) — inverse: emits canonical query string from params
- `build_local_href()` (line 150) — builds full `/local/<slug>?query` path
- `build_rule_set()` (line 161) — converts `LocalRulesParams` → engine `RuleSet`

**Extensibility**: `LocalRulesParams` is the intermediate struct where mode choices would be injected; `parse_local_rules()` and `build_local_query()` would parse/emit new `mode=` and `ai_side=` / `ai_difficulty=` params.

---

### 2. **Local Play Page (`pages/local.rs`)**

**File**: `/Users/david/Documents/Program/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs` (254 lines)

**GameState & signal flow** (lines 52-90):
- `state: RwSignal<GameState>` — central game state (line 54)
- `selected: RwSignal<Option<Square>>` — user's selected piece for move (line 55)
- `view: Signal<PlayerView>` — derived from current `state`, projected for side-to-move (line 63)
- Effect at line 71: clears selection when side flips

**Move processing** (lines 91-177):
- `on_click: Callback<Square>` (line 105) — click handler
  - Checks chain mode (lines 114-136)
  - Searches legal moves via `find_move()` (line 146, imported from `crate::state`)
  - Updates state: `state.update(|s| { s.make_move(&mv).expect(...); s.refresh_status(); })` (line 147)
  - Clears selection after move (line 151)
- Chain mode auto-ends via `on_end_chain` (line 91)
- Undo via `on_undo` (line 179)

**Pass-and-play turn alternation** (line 60):
- Fixed observer: `observer = Side::RED` (board never flips)
- Legal moves filtered by `side_to_move` via `PlayerView::project(s, s.side_to_move)` (line 63)
- Side signal auto-clears selection on flip (line 71)

**Board integration** (lines 209-215):
- Props: `shape`, `observer`, `view`, `selected`, `on_click` callback
- Board emits `on_click` directly (no filtering or "disabled" mode yet)

**Status display** (lines 224-242):
- `<Show when=wip>` renders `wip-overlay` (full-screen blocker with `pointer-events: auto` on inner banner)
- Uses `EndOverlay` (conditional, line 238) for victory/draw
- Uses `CapturedStrip` (line 241) for piece trophies

---

### 3. **Board Component (`components/board.rs`)**

**File**: `/Users/david/Documents/Program/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/clients/chess-web/src/components/board.rs` (263 lines)

**Props** (lines 22-28):
```rust
#[component]
pub fn Board(
    shape: BoardShape,
    observer: Side,
    #[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>,
    #[prop(into)] selected: Signal<Option<Square>>,
    #[prop(into)] on_click: Callback<Square>,
) -> impl IntoView
```
- **No "disabled" / "read-only" prop**. Click events are always emitted to `on_click`.
- To add read-only mode, would need a new optional `#[prop(default = false)] disabled: bool` parameter

**SVG structure** (lines 35-55):
- Overlay layer renders move-dots via `legal_targets()` (line 42)
- Pieces layer (line 44)
- Overlay-top layer: chain-lock + selection markers (lines 47-49)
- **Hit-cell layer** (line 51-53) — `hit_cells()` renders transparent `<rect>` per square with `on:pointerup` → `on_click.call(sq)`

**Move-input flow**:
1. User clicks cell → `on:pointerup` fires `on_click.call(sq)`
2. `local.rs` receives callback, searches `legal_moves` (line 146)
3. If found, applies move and updates state
4. If not found, toggles selection if piece is selectable

**No affordances exist for disabling board interaction** — would require:
- Adding `disabled: bool` param to `Board`
- Wrapping hit-cell layer in `<Show when=move || !disabled>`
- Or conditionally setting `pointer-events: none` on the SVG or cells

---

### 4. **Picker Page (`pages/picker.rs`)**

**File**: `/Users/david/Documents/Program/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs` (200+ lines)

- `seed_text: RwSignal<String>` (line 86)
- `house()` closure (line 88) — accumulates flags from individual checkboxes
- `apply_preset()` (line 107) — sets all flags from a preset

**URL generation** (lines 45-47, 114-118):
- `href()` closure rebuilds link via `build_local_href()` on every signal change

**Key patterns**:

- **chess-ai is NOT yet a dependency** — would need to add: `chess-ai = { path = "../../crates/chess-ai" }` (likely not wasm32-gated, since the AI needs to run in-bundle)

**Async model** (ws.rs lines 50, 69):
- Uses `wasm_bindgen_futures::spawn_local()` for background tasks (websocket read/write pumps)
- Pattern available for AI computation: `spawn_local(async move { ... })`

---

### Summary — Integration touchpoints for VS AI:

| Component | File | Integration point | Current affordance |
|-----------|------|-------------------|-------------------|
| **Routing** | routes.rs | Extend `LocalRulesParams` + `parse_local_rules()` / `build_local_query()` | Clean enum-based pattern; add `mode` field |
| **Picker form** | picker.rs | Add mode radio + conditional AI fieldset | Signal-driven; easy to add new form sections |
| **Local page** | local.rs | Gate AI move generation on `mode`; spawn async AI task when player moves | Signal flow exists; `spawn_local()` available |
| **Board** | board.rs | Add optional `disabled: bool` prop to suppress interaction when AI plays | Currently no gating; would need `<Show>` wrapper on hit-cell layer |
| **Sidebar** | sidebar.rs | Add "AI thinking..." / "AI side" status line | `.check-badge` pattern reusable; status text slot available |
| **Dependencies** | Cargo.toml | Add `chess-ai` to main dependencies | Workspace member; straightforward include |

No web-worker infrastructure needed — `spawn_local()` is sufficient for short think times. If deep search locks the main thread, a dedicated worker thread can be spun up later.

## Summary: Rust + WASM Xiangqi AI Integration Plan

### 1. **chess-ai Crate Status**

**File**: `/Users/david/Documents/Program/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/crates/chess-ai/Cargo.toml`

- **Stub stage**: Empty `lib.rs` with one-liner comment.
- **Dependencies**: Only `chess-core` and `chess-engine` (both sibling crates, both also stubs).
- **No blocker deps**: No non-WASM dependencies currently added; `chess-core`'s target-specific `getrandom = { features = ["js"] }` is already in place for browser builds.

### 2. **chess-core API Surface for AI**

**Key functions and types**:

| Capability | Location | API Signature |
|---|---|---|
| **Enumerate legal moves** | `crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:86` | `pub fn legal_moves(&self) -> MoveList` returns `SmallVec<[Move; 32]>` |
| **Apply move** | `crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:104` | `pub fn make_move(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError>` (mutates in-place, fills `Move::Reveal` payload) |
| **Undo move** | `crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:146` | `pub fn unmake_move(&mut self) -> Result<(), CoreError>` (pops history, mutates board back) |
| **Game status** | `crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:479` | `pub fn refresh_status(&mut self)` (updates `self.status: GameStatus`) — **must call after move** |
| **Status enum** | `crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:20` | `enum GameStatus { Ongoing, Won { winner, reason }, Drawn { reason } }` |
| **Side to move** | `crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:59` | `pub side_to_move: Side` accessor on `GameState` |
| **Board access** | `crates/chess-core/src/board/mod.rs:63` | `pub fn get(&self, sq: Square) -> Option<PieceOnSquare>` + `pub fn squares(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = Square>` |
| **Piece values** | `crates/chess-core/src/piece.rs:53` | `pub const fn banqi_rank(self) -> u8` (General=6 down to Soldier=0) — **only for banqi capture rules, no material eval yet** |
| **Move enum** | `crates/chess-core/src/moves.rs:19` | Variants: `Reveal`, `Step`, `Capture`, `ChainCapture`, `CannonJump`, `DarkCapture`, `EndChain` |

**Critical gotchas**:
- `make_move` does **not** auto-call `refresh_status()` (line 114 in CLAUDE.md); clones state in xiangqi's legality filter to avoid recursion.
- Move generation clones state once per move candidate for xiangqi strict mode (line 110 in CLAUDE.md) — fine for positions up to 32 moves.
- `banqi_rank()` is for capture precedence only (e.g., Chariot beats Soldier); no material-value table exists yet.

### 3. **Banqi Reveal Mechanics**

**File**: `crates/chess-core/src/moves.rs:19–45`

- `Move::Reveal { at: Square, revealed: Option<Piece> }` — client sends `None`, server/engine fills `Some(piece)` post-flip.
- For AI: reveal nodes are deterministic (not hidden) during tree search *after* the move is made (the engine fills `revealed` synchronously in `make_move`, line 113 in `state/mod.rs`).
- `Move::DarkCapture` (reveal + capture atomic): also `revealed: Option<Piece>` and `attacker: Option<Piece>` filled server-side.
- **Banqi information set**: Full board state is deterministic once moves are played; no need for expectimax over unknown tiles within a search subtree—the AI sees the board as it is.

### 4. **WASM Readiness**

**Confirmed buildable** (per CLAUDE.md line 31):
```bash
cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-core
```
- `chess-core` has **no platform-specific deps** except the target-conditional `getrandom = { features = ["js"] }` for browser WASM.
- `chess-engine` and `chess-ai` currently have zero dependencies beyond `chess-core`, so they inherit its WASM-cleanness.
- **Required one-time setup**: `rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown` (line 79 in CLAUDE.md).

### Design Inventory Summary

| Item | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Move enumeration | ✓ Ready | `legal_moves()` + `MoveList` small-vec, clones during legality check in xiangqi only |
| Make/unmake | ✓ Ready | In-place mutation; `unmake_move` pops history, `make_move` does NOT refresh status |
| Status detection | ✓ Ready | Call `refresh_status()` after each move; covers checkmate, stalemate, no-progress draw |
| Board reading | ✓ Ready | `board.get(square)` + `board.squares()` iterator; no high-level material eval |
| Piece identity | ✓ Ready | `PieceKind::ALL` enum (7 kinds) + `banqi_rank()` precedence; **no value table** |
| WASM-safe | ✓ Ready | `chess-core` + `chess-ai`/`chess-engine` stubs are WASM-clean |
| Banqi reveals | ⚠️ Deterministic | No expectimax needed—board state is fully observable post-move |

**Next steps for AI impl**: Define evaluation heuristics (material, position, tempo) in `chess-engine`, wire alpha-beta search (or ISMCTS for banqi hidden info pre-reveal), and expose a `choose_move(state, depth) -> Move` API to both chess-tui and chess-web (WASM target).

```md
# Plan: Local "vs AI" mode (alpha-beta MVP)

## Context

Today the chess-web app supports only pass-and-play (`/local/:variant`) and online play via chess-net. The `chess-ai` crate is a stub. Per `docs/ai-deep-research-report.md` and the user's research, the MVP should be a **clean-room, lightweight alpha-beta engine** running in-process (WASM) — not RL, not Pikafish, not LLM, not even a Web Worker yet. Goal: a player can pick "vs AI" + side + difficulty in the picker, and play locally against a deterministic search engine that plugs into the existing legal-move pipeline.

Scope: **xiangqi only in Phase 1.** Banqi has hidden-tile information asymmetry that this minimax design would silently break (see Risks). Three-kingdom banqi has no engine at all.

## Approach

### Phase 1A — `crates/chess-ai` engine (MVP)

Single new module producing one public function:

\```rust
pub fn choose_move(state: &GameState, opts: &AiOptions) -> Option<AiMoveResult>;

pub struct AiOptions { pub difficulty: Difficulty, pub max_depth: Option<u8>, pub randomness: u8 /* 0..=255 */, pub seed: Option<u64> }
pub enum Difficulty { Easy, Normal, Hard }
pub struct AiMoveResult { pub mv: Move, pub score: i32, pub depth: u8, pub nodes: u32, pub elapsed_ms: u32 }
\```

Implementation:

- **Search**: negamax + alpha-beta, no transposition table, no quiescence, no iterative deepening (Phase 2 candidates).
- **Move generation**: call `state.legal_moves()` directly (`crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:86`). The xiangqi clone-on-probe legality cost is acceptable for depth ≤ 4.
- **Make/unmake**: `state.make_move(&mv)` + `state.unmake_move()` (`state/mod.rs:104,146`). Mutates in place — no per-node cloning needed for the search itself (cloning happens inside `legal_moves` only, and only in xiangqi strict).
- **Terminal detection**: at leaf, run `state.refresh_status()` once; if `Won { winner }` return `±MATE - depth`; `Drawn` return `0`. CLAUDE.md flags that `make_move` does NOT auto-refresh — we must do it.
- **Evaluation** (xiangqi handcrafted): material table (Chariot 900, Cannon 450, Horse 400, Advisor/Elephant 200, Soldier 100 / 200 past river, General excluded from material — checkmate already handled as ±MATE). Side-relative score: `eval(state) = sum(red_material) - sum(black_material)` then signed for `state.side_to_move`. Iterate `board.squares()` (`board/mod.rs`) to read pieces. No PSTs in MVP — single-line "soldier crossed river" bonus only.
- **Move ordering**: captures first (cheap — match on `Move::Capture` / `Move::CannonJump` variants), then quiet. Skip MVV-LVA for now.
- **Difficulty mapping**:
  - Easy: depth 1, pick uniformly from top-3 by score (deterministic with `seed`).
  - Normal: depth 3, pick best with small ±10cp tiebreak randomness.
  - Hard: depth 4, strict best.
- **Fallback**: if `legal_moves()` is empty → `None` (caller treats as game-over). If something panics-worthy comes up, return a random legal move rather than the engine's pick.
- **Determinism**: use `rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64` (already used by `chess-core` for banqi shuffles per CLAUDE.md). No `thread_rng`. Keeps the engine WASM-clean and reproducible.

Tests (`crates/chess-ai/tests/`):
1. Initial xiangqi position returns a legal move at each difficulty.
2. Mate-in-1 fixture (use `.pos` DSL via `GameState::from_pos_text` per CLAUDE.md): hard mode picks the mate.
3. Forced-recapture fixture: hard mode plays the recapture.
4. No-legal-moves: returns `None` without panicking.
5. Determinism: same `(state, seed, difficulty)` → same move.

### Phase 1B — chess-web wiring

**`Cargo.toml`** (`clients/chess-web/Cargo.toml`): add `chess-ai = { path = "../../crates/chess-ai" }` to base deps (not target-gated — the engine must compile native too for any future shared tests).

**Routing** (`clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs`):
- Extend `LocalRulesParams` (line 95) with `mode: PlayMode`, `ai_side: Side`, `ai_difficulty: Difficulty`. Tokens: `?mode=ai&ai=red|black&diff=easy|normal|hard`. Default `mode=pvp` keeps existing URLs byte-identical (back-compat).
- Update `parse_local_rules` (line 113) and `build_local_query` (line 125) — pure-logic, native-tested.

**Picker** (`clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs`):
- Add a `mode: RwSignal<PlayMode>` radio at the top of `XiangqiCard` (line 43). When `VsAi`, reveal a small fieldset: side radio (red/black), difficulty `<select>`. Banqi/three-kingdom cards: hide the toggle entirely (xiangqi-only in MVP).

**Local page** (`clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs`):
- Read `mode`/`ai_side`/`ai_difficulty` from the parsed params.
- Add `ai_thinking: RwSignal<bool>`.
- After every `state.update(...)` that lands a player move (line 147 site), check `mode == VsAi && side_to_move == ai_side && status == Ongoing`. If so, set `ai_thinking=true`, `spawn_local` an async task that:
  1. `set_timeout` ~250ms (cosmetic delay, keeps "AI thinking" visible),
  2. snapshots the current `state.get_untracked()`,
  3. calls `chess_ai::choose_move(&snap, &opts)`,
  4. on the main task, re-checks the state hasn't changed (token/epoch counter — increment on every undo/reset/move), then `state.update(|s| { s.make_move(&ai_mv).unwrap(); s.refresh_status(); })`,
  5. clears `ai_thinking`.
- Cancellation: bump a `move_epoch: RwSignal<u32>` on undo/reset/restart; the AI task captures the epoch at start and discards its result if the epoch changed. Avoids stale moves landing after the player undid.

**Board** (`clients/chess-web/src/components/board.rs`):
- Add `#[prop(default = false)] disabled: bool`. Wrap the hit-cell layer (line 51) in `<Show when=move || !disabled>`. Local page passes `disabled = ai_thinking.get() || (mode == VsAi && side_to_move != player_side)` to prevent the user from clicking during AI's turn.

**Sidebar** (`clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs`):
- New `<Show when=ai_thinking>` row above the turn label (line 98) reusing `.check-badge` styling: text "AI thinking…". When idle in VsAi mode, append "(vs AI — Hard)" to the turn label.

### Phase 1C — chess-tui (deferred — not in this PR)

Single-player AI in chess-tui is a natural follow-up but not in scope; record as a TODO entry. Engine API is reusable from native targets.

## Critical files

- `crates/chess-ai/Cargo.toml` — add `rand`, `rand_chacha`; depend on `chess-core` only.
- `crates/chess-ai/src/lib.rs` — `choose_move`, `negamax`, `evaluate`, `Difficulty`, `AiOptions`.
- `crates/chess-ai/tests/{smoke,mate_in_one,determinism}.rs` — fixtures under `tests/fixtures/xiangqi/`.
- `clients/chess-web/Cargo.toml` — add `chess-ai` dep.
- `clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs` — `LocalRulesParams` + parser/builder (pure-logic, native tests).
- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs` — mode/side/difficulty controls (xiangqi card only).
- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs` — AI-driven move pump, epoch-cancellation, disabled gating.
- `clients/chess-web/src/components/board.rs` — `disabled` prop.
- `clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs` — "AI thinking…" indicator.
- `TODO.md` — record banqi-AI follow-up + chess-tui vs-AI follow-up.

## Risks

- **Banqi must NOT use this engine in MVP.** `Move::Reveal { revealed: None }` becomes `Some(true_piece)` inside `make_move` — a minimax that searches reveal nodes effectively *cheats* by seeing future tiles. Proper banqi AI needs determinization / ISMCTS. Picker hides the toggle on the banqi card; local page ignores `mode=ai` when variant != xiangqi.
- **Hard mode at depth 4 may exceed ~1s on slower phones.** Mitigation: cap `nodes` at e.g. 200k and short-circuit; or drop hard to depth 3 if benchmarking shows trouble. Web Worker is Phase 2 (`backlog/`).
- **Stale AI move after undo.** Mitigated by `move_epoch` token check before applying.
- **No threefold-repetition handling** in the engine — chess-core itself doesn't implement it (CLAUDE.md). Engine may oscillate in drawn endgames; acceptable for MVP, document in `pitfalls/` if it surfaces.
- **Casual xiangqi** (`xiangqi_allow_self_check`) — the AI calls `legal_moves()` which respects the rule set, so it'll happily blunder into self-check in casual mode. That's actually the desired UX (matches what humans can do).

## Approved Plan:
# Plan: Local "vs AI" mode (alpha-beta MVP)

## Context

Today the chess-web app supports only pass-and-play (`/local/:variant`) and online play via chess-net. The `chess-ai` crate is a stub. Per `docs/ai-deep-research-report.md` and the user's research, the MVP should be a **clean-room, lightweight alpha-beta engine** running in-process (WASM) — not RL, not Pikafish, not LLM, not even a Web Worker yet. Goal: a player can pick "vs AI" + side + difficulty in the picker, and play locally against a deterministic search engine that plugs into the existing legal-move pipeline.

Scope: **xiangqi only in Phase 1.** Banqi has hidden-tile information asymmetry that this minimax design would silently break (see Risks). Three-kingdom banqi has no engine at all.

## Approach

### Phase 1A — `crates/chess-ai` engine (MVP)

Single new module producing one public function:

```rust
pub fn choose_move(state: &GameState, opts: &AiOptions) -> Option<AiMoveResult>;

pub struct AiOptions { pub difficulty: Difficulty, pub max_depth: Option<u8>, pub randomness: u8 /* 0..=255 */, pub seed: Option<u64> }
pub enum Difficulty { Easy, Normal, Hard }
pub struct AiMoveResult { pub mv: Move, pub score: i32, pub depth: u8, pub nodes: u32, pub elapsed_ms: u32 }
```

Implementation:

- **Search**: negamax + alpha-beta, no transposition table, no quiescence, no iterative deepening (Phase 2 candidates).
- **Move generation**: call `state.legal_moves()` directly (`crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:86`). The xiangqi clone-on-probe legality cost is acceptable for depth ≤ 4.
- **Make/unmake**: `state.make_move(&mv)` + `state.unmake_move()` (`state/mod.rs:104,146`). Mutates in place — no per-node cloning needed for the search itself (cloning happens inside `legal_moves` only, and only in xiangqi strict).
- **Terminal detection**: at leaf, run `state.refresh_status()` once; if `Won { winner }` return `±MATE - depth`; `Drawn` return `0`. CLAUDE.md flags that `make_move` does NOT auto-refresh — we must do it.
- **Evaluation** (xiangqi handcrafted): material table (Chariot 900, Cannon 450, Horse 400, Advisor/Elephant 200, Soldier 100 / 200 past river, General excluded from material — checkmate already handled as ±MATE). Side-relative score: `eval(state) = sum(red_material) - sum(black_material)` then signed for `state.side_to_move`. Iterate `board.squares()` (`board/mod.rs`) to read pieces. No PSTs in MVP — single-line "soldier crossed river" bonus only.
- **Move ordering**: captures first (cheap — match on `Move::Capture` / `Move::CannonJump` variants), then quiet. Skip MVV-LVA for now.
- **Difficulty mapping**:
  - Easy: depth 1, pick uniformly from top-3 by score (deterministic with `seed`).
  - Normal: depth 3, pick best with small ±10cp tiebreak randomness.
  - Hard: depth 4, strict best.
- **Fallback**: if `legal_moves()` is empty → `None` (caller treats as game-over). If something panics-worthy comes up, return a random legal move rather than the engine's pick.
- **Determinism**: use `rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64` (already used by `chess-core` for banqi shuffles per CLAUDE.md). No `thread_rng`. Keeps the engine WASM-clean and reproducible.

Tests (`crates/chess-ai/tests/`):
1. Initial xiangqi position returns a legal move at each difficulty.
2. Mate-in-1 fixture (use `.pos` DSL via `GameState::from_pos_text` per CLAUDE.md): hard mode picks the mate.
3. Forced-recapture fixture: hard mode plays the recapture.
4. No-legal-moves: returns `None` without panicking.
5. Determinism: same `(state, seed, difficulty)` → same move.

### Phase 1B — chess-web wiring

**`Cargo.toml`** (`clients/chess-web/Cargo.toml`): add `chess-ai = { path = "../../crates/chess-ai" }` to base deps (not target-gated — the engine must compile native too for any future shared tests).

**Routing** (`clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs`):
- Extend `LocalRulesParams` (line 95) with `mode: PlayMode`, `ai_side: Side`, `ai_difficulty: Difficulty`. Tokens: `?mode=ai&ai=red|black&diff=easy|normal|hard`. Default `mode=pvp` keeps existing URLs byte-identical (back-compat).
- Update `parse_local_rules` (line 113) and `build_local_query` (line 125) — pure-logic, native-tested.

**Picker** (`clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs`):

**Local page** (`clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs`):
- Read `mode`/`ai_side`/`ai_difficulty` from the parsed params.
- Add `ai_thinking: RwSignal<bool>`.
- After every `state.update(...)` that lands a player move (line 147 site), check `mode == VsAi && side_to_move == ai_side && status == Ongoing`. If so, set `ai_thinking=true`, `spawn_local` an async task that:
  1. `set_timeout` ~250ms (cosmetic delay, keeps "AI thinking" visible),
  2. snapshots the current `state.get_untracked()`,
  3. calls `chess_ai::choose_move(&snap, &opts)`,
  4. on the main task, re-checks the state hasn't changed (token/epoch counter — increment on every undo/reset/move), then `state.update(|s| { s.make_move(&ai_mv).unwrap(); s.refresh_status(); })`,
  5. clears `ai_thinking`.
- Cancellation: bump a `move_epoch: RwSignal<u32>` on undo/reset/restart; the AI task captures the epoch at start and discards its result if the epoch changed. Avoids stale moves landing after the player undid.

**Board** (`clients/chess-web/src/components/board.rs`):
- Add `#[prop(default = false)] disabled: bool`. Wrap the hit-cell layer (line 51) in `<Show when=move || !disabled>`. Local page passes `disabled = ai_thinking.get() || (mode == VsAi && side_to_move != player_side)` to prevent the user from clicking during AI's turn.

**Sidebar** (`clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs`):

### Phase 1C — chess-tui (deferred — not in this PR)

Single-player AI in chess-tui is a natural follow-up but not in scope; record as a TODO entry. Engine API is reusable from native targets.

## Risks

- **Banqi must NOT use this engine in MVP.** `Move::Reveal { revealed: None }` becomes `Some(true_piece)` inside `make_move` — a minimax that searches reveal nodes effectively *cheats* by seeing future tiles. Proper banqi AI needs determinization / ISMCTS. Picker hides the toggle on the banqi card; local page ignores `mode=ai` when variant != xiangqi.
- **Hard mode at depth 4 may exceed ~1s on slower phones.** Mitigation: cap `nodes` at e.g. 200k and short-circuit; or drop hard to depth 3 if benchmarking shows trouble. Web Worker is Phase 2 (`backlog/`).
- **Stale AI move after undo.** Mitigated by `move_epoch` token check before applying.
- **No threefold-repetition handling** in the engine — chess-core itself doesn't implement it (CLAUDE.md). Engine may oscillate in drawn endgames; acceptable for MVP, document in `pitfalls/` if it surfaces.

14	use crate::error::CoreError;

19	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
20	pub enum GameStatus {
21	    Ongoing,
22	    Won { winner: Side, reason: WinReason },
23	    Drawn { reason: DrawReason },

26	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
27	pub enum WinReason {
28	    Checkmate,
29	    Stalemate,
30	    Resignation,
31	    OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
32	    Timeout,
33	    /// Casual xiangqi (`xiangqi_allow_self_check`): the loser's general was
34	    /// physically captured rather than ending in checkmate. In standard
35	    /// rules this state is unreachable because the legality filter rejects
36	    /// any move that would leave the general capturable.
37	    GeneralCaptured,
38	}
39	
40	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
41	pub enum DrawReason {
42	    NoProgress,
43	    Repetition,
44	    Agreed,
45	    InsufficientMaterial,
46	}
47	
48	/// Side ↔ piece-color assignment for banqi (set after the first flip).
49	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
50	pub struct SideAssignment {
51	    /// `mapping[side.0 as usize]` = the piece-color that seat controls.
52	    pub mapping: SmallVec<[Side; 3]>,
53	}
54	
55	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
56	pub struct GameState {
57	    pub rules: RuleSet,
58	    pub board: Board,
59	    pub side_to_move: Side,
60	    pub turn_order: TurnOrder,
61	    pub history: Vec<MoveRecord>,
62	    pub status: GameStatus,
63	    /// Banqi only: established after first flip. `None` until then.
64	    pub side_assignment: Option<SideAssignment>,
65	    pub no_progress_plies: u16,
66	    /// Banqi 連吃 chain-mode lock. `Some(sq)` means the moving piece at
67	    /// `sq` just made a chain-eligible capture and may continue capturing
68	    /// — the turn does NOT auto-advance until the player issues
69	    /// `Move::EndChain { at: sq }` (or makes another chain-eligible
70	    /// capture which keeps the lock active). `#[serde(default)]` so
71	    /// pre-chain-mode snapshots load.
72	    #[serde(default)]
73	    pub chain_lock: Option<crate::coord::Square>,
74	}
75	
76	impl GameState {
77	    pub fn new(rules: RuleSet) -> Self {
78	        crate::setup::build_initial_state(rules)
79	    }
80	
81	    /// Generate all legal moves for the side to move.
82	    ///
83	    /// When `chain_lock` is set (banqi 連吃 mid-chain), the legal-move
84	    /// list is filtered to capture-only moves originating from the
85	    /// locked square, plus `Move::EndChain` as the explicit terminator.
86	    pub fn legal_moves(&self) -> MoveList {
87	        let mut out = MoveList::new();
88	        crate::rules::generate_moves(self, &mut out);
89	        if let Some(lock) = self.chain_lock {
90	            out.retain(|m| match m {
91	                Move::Capture { from, .. }
92	                | Move::CannonJump { from, .. }
93	                | Move::DarkCapture { from, .. }
94	                | Move::ChainCapture { from, .. } => *from == lock,
95	                _ => false,
96	            });
97	            out.push(Move::EndChain { at: lock });
98	        }
99	        out
100	    }
101	
102	    /// Apply a move. Caller is responsible for legality (use `legal_moves`).
103	    /// Returns the move pushed to history (with `Reveal` payloads filled in).
104	    pub fn make_move(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
105	        if matches!(self.status, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) {
106	            return Err(CoreError::GameOver);
107	        }
108	        let mover = self.side_to_move;
109	        let no_progress_before = self.no_progress_plies;
110	        let chain_lock_before = self.chain_lock;
111	
112	        // Normalize the move (fill in Reveal payload from board if missing).
113	        let normalized = self.normalize_for_apply(m)?;
114	        self.apply_inner(&normalized)?;
115	
116	        self.history.push(MoveRecord {
117	            mover,
118	            the_move: normalized.clone(),
119	            no_progress_before,
120	            chain_lock_before,
121	        });
122	
123	        if normalized.resets_no_progress() {
124	            self.no_progress_plies = 0;
125	        } else {
126	            self.no_progress_plies = self.no_progress_plies.saturating_add(1);
127	        }
128	
129	        // Decide whether the moving piece enters / continues chain mode.
130	        // Banqi-only, gated on CHAIN_CAPTURE flag, only after a real
131	        // capture that landed an attacker on a square where it can
132	        // capture again. EndChain explicitly clears the lock.
133	        self.chain_lock = self.compute_chain_lock_after(&normalized);
134	
135	        // Turn advances UNLESS we just entered / are continuing chain
136	        // mode (chain_lock now Some).
137	        if self.chain_lock.is_none() {
138	            self.turn_order.advance();
139	            self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
140	        }
141	
142	        Ok(())
143	    }
144	
145	    /// Undo the last move.
146	    pub fn unmake_move(&mut self) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
147	        let rec = self.history.pop().ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("no move to undo"))?;
148	        self.unapply_inner(&rec.the_move)?;
149	        self.no_progress_plies = rec.no_progress_before;
150	
151	        // If chain_lock is currently Some, the move did NOT advance the
152	        // turn (the player was either entering or continuing chain mode).
153	        // Otherwise the turn advanced and we need to rewind it.
154	        let turn_advanced = self.chain_lock.is_none();
155	
156	        // Restore the pre-move chain_lock.
157	        self.chain_lock = rec.chain_lock_before;
158	
159	        if turn_advanced {
160	            if self.turn_order.current == 0 {
161	                self.turn_order.current = (self.turn_order.seats.len() as u8) - 1;
162	            } else {
163	                self.turn_order.current -= 1;
164	            }
165	            self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
166	        }
167	        // Game status reset to Ongoing — caller may recompute.
168	        self.status = GameStatus::Ongoing;
169	        Ok(())
170	    }
171	
172	    /// After applying `m`, decide whether the moving piece is now in
173	    /// banqi 連吃 chain mode. Returns the locked piece's square when
174	    /// chain mode should remain / activate; `None` otherwise.
175	    ///
176	    /// Activates only for banqi + `CHAIN_CAPTURE` rules, after a capture
177	    /// move whose attacker actually landed on a square (probe and trade
178	    /// outcomes of `Move::DarkCapture` skip the lock — the attacker
179	    /// stayed put or died). The attacker must have at least one further
180	    /// legal capture from the landing square.
181	    fn compute_chain_lock_after(&self, m: &Move) -> Option<crate::coord::Square> {
182	        if self.rules.variant != Variant::Banqi {
183	            return None;
184	        }
185	        if !self.rules.house.contains(crate::rules::HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE) {
186	            return None;
187	        }
188	        // Where did the moving piece end up? Only chain-eligible when
189	        // the attacker actually moved to a new square.
190	        let landing = match m {
191	            Move::Capture { to, .. } | Move::CannonJump { to, .. } => *to,
192	            Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker } => {
193	                // `Move::DarkCapture` resolves to one of three outcomes
194	                // at apply-time. Only `Capture` (rank ok, attacker took
195	                // defender's square) puts the attacker at `to`; `Probe`
196	                // leaves the attacker at `from` and `Trade` removes the
197	                // attacker entirely. Neither of those is chain-eligible.
198	                let attacker_piece = (*attacker)?;
199	                let revealed_piece = (*revealed)?;
200	                match dark_capture_outcome(
201	                    attacker_piece,
202	                    revealed_piece,
203	                    self.rules.house,
204	                    *from,
205	                    *to,
206	                    &self.board,
207	                ) {
208	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => *to,
209	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe | DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade => return None,
210	                }
211	            }
212	            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => path.last().map(|h| h.to)?,
213	            // Reveal / Step / EndChain don't trigger chain mode.
214	            Move::Reveal { .. } | Move::Step { .. } | Move::EndChain { .. } => return None,
215	        };
216	        let landing_pos = self.board.get(landing)?;
217	        if !landing_pos.revealed {
218	            return None;
219	        }
220	        let active_color = self.current_color();
221	        if landing_pos.piece.side != active_color {
222	            // Defensive: shouldn't happen now that DarkCapture branches
223	            // explicitly above, but stays correct if future variants add
224	            // new capture-with-reveal moves.
225	            return None;
226	        }
227	        if has_capture_from(self, landing, landing_pos.piece) {
228	            Some(landing)
229	        } else {
230	            None
231	        }
232	    }
233	
234	    fn normalize_for_apply(&self, m: &Move) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
235	        match m {
236	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None } => {
237	                let pos = self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: empty"))?;
238	                if pos.revealed {
239	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: already revealed"));
240	                }
241	                Ok(Move::Reveal { at: *at, revealed: Some(pos.piece) })
242	            }
243	            Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker } => {
244	                let attacker = match attacker {
245	                    Some(p) => *p,
246	                    None => {
247	                        let pos = self
248	                            .board
249	                            .get(*from)
250	                            .ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: empty from"))?;
251	                        if !pos.revealed {
252	                            return Err(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: from not revealed"));
253	                        }
254	                        pos.piece
255	                    }
256	                };
257	                let revealed = match revealed {
258	                    Some(p) => *p,
259	                    None => {
260	                        let pos = self
261	                            .board
262	                            .get(*to)
263	                            .ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: empty to"))?;
264	                        if pos.revealed {
265	                            return Err(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: target already revealed"));
266	                        }
267	                        pos.piece
268	                    }
269	                };
270	                Ok(Move::DarkCapture {
271	                    from: *from,
272	                    to: *to,
273	                    revealed: Some(revealed),
274	                    attacker: Some(attacker),
275	                })
276	            }
277	            _ => Ok(m.clone()),
278	        }
279	    }
280	
281	    fn apply_inner(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
282	        match m {
283	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed } => {
284	                let mut pos = self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: empty"))?;
285	                if pos.revealed {
286	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: already revealed"));
287	                }
288	                pos.revealed = true;
289	                self.board.set(*at, Some(pos));
290	                // First flip in banqi locks side assignment.
291	                if self.side_assignment.is_none() && self.rules.variant == Variant::Banqi {
292	                    if let Some(p) = revealed {
293	                        self.side_assignment =
294	                            Some(banqi_side_assignment(self.side_to_move, p.side));
295	                    }
296	                }
297	            }
298	            Move::Step { from, to } => {
299	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Step: empty from"))?;
300	                if self.board.get(*to).is_some() {
301	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Step: occupied to"));
302	                }
303	                self.board.set(*from, None);
304	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
305	            }
306	            Move::Capture { from, to, captured: _ } => {
307	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Capture: empty from"))?;
308	                self.board.set(*from, None);
309	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
310	            }
311	            Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen: _, captured: _ } => {
312	                let p =
313	                    self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("CannonJump: empty from"))?;
314	                self.board.set(*from, None);
315	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
316	            }
317	            Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
318	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Chain: empty from"))?;
319	                if path.is_empty() {
320	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Chain: empty path"));
321	                }
322	                self.board.set(*from, None);
323	                for hop in &path[..path.len() - 1] {
324	                    self.board.set(hop.to, None);
325	                }
326	                self.board.set(path.last().unwrap().to, Some(p));
327	            }
328	            Move::EndChain { at } => {
329	                // No board change — just verifies that a chain-locked piece
330	                // exists at `at`. The turn-advance machinery in
331	                // `make_move` clears `chain_lock` based on
332	                // `compute_chain_lock_after`.
333	                if self.chain_lock != Some(*at) {
334	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("EndChain: no active chain lock"));
335	                }
336	            }
337	            Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker } => {
338	                let revealed_piece =
339	                    revealed.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: missing revealed"))?;
340	                let attacker_piece =
341	                    attacker.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: missing attacker"))?;
342	                // Reveal the target.
343	                let mut target_pos =
344	                    self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: empty to"))?;
345	                if target_pos.revealed {
346	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: target already revealed"));
347	                }
348	                target_pos.revealed = true;
349	                self.board.set(*to, Some(target_pos));
350	
351	                // First flip in banqi locks side assignment (mirrors Reveal).
352	                if self.side_assignment.is_none() && self.rules.variant == Variant::Banqi {
353	                    self.side_assignment =
354	                        Some(banqi_side_assignment(self.side_to_move, revealed_piece.side));
355	                }
356	
357	                // Resolve outcome based on rank + DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE flag.
358	                let attacker_pos =
359	                    self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: empty from"))?;
360	                let outcome = dark_capture_outcome(
361	                    attacker_piece,
362	                    revealed_piece,
363	                    self.rules.house,
364	                    *from,
365	                    *to,
366	                    &self.board,
367	                );
368	                match outcome {
369	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => {
370	                        self.board.set(*from, None);
371	                        self.board.set(*to, Some(attacker_pos));
372	                    }
373	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade => {
374	                        // Attacker dies; target stays revealed at `to`.
375	                        self.board.set(*from, None);
376	                    }
377	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe => {
378	                        // Both pieces stay; target is now revealed.
379	                    }
380	                }
381	            }
382	        }
383	        Ok(())
384	    }
385	
386	    fn unapply_inner(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
387	        match m {
388	            Move::Reveal { at, .. } => {
389	                let mut pos =
390	                    self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Reveal: empty"))?;
391	                pos.revealed = false;
392	                self.board.set(*at, Some(pos));
393	                // First-flip might have set side_assignment; if this was the first move, clear it.
394	                if self.history.is_empty() {
395	                    self.side_assignment = None;
396	                }
397	            }
398	            Move::Step { from, to } => {
399	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Step: empty to"))?;
400	                self.board.set(*to, None);
401	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
402	            }
403	            Move::Capture { from, to, captured } => {
404	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Capture: empty to"))?;
405	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(*captured)));
406	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
407	            }
408	            Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen: _, captured } => {
409	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Jump: empty to"))?;
410	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(*captured)));
411	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
412	            }
413	            Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
414	                let last_to = path.last().unwrap().to;
415	                let p =
416	                    self.board.get(last_to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Chain: empty last"))?;
417	                self.board.set(last_to, None);
418	                // Restore intermediate captures (in any order).
419	                for hop in path {
420	                    self.board.set(hop.to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(hop.captured)));
421	                }
422	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
423	            }
424	            Move::EndChain { .. } => {
425	                // No board change to undo. chain_lock is restored by
426	                // `unmake_move` from `MoveRecord.chain_lock_before`.
427	            }
428	            Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker } => {
429	                let revealed_piece =
430	                    revealed.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo DarkCapture: missing revealed"))?;
431	                let attacker_piece =
432	                    attacker.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo DarkCapture: missing attacker"))?;
433	                let outcome = dark_capture_outcome(
434	                    attacker_piece,
435	                    revealed_piece,
436	                    self.rules.house,
437	                    *from,
438	                    *to,
439	                    &self.board,
440	                );
441	                // Restore attacker at `from` (reveals it as it was).
442	                match outcome {
443	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => {
444	                        // Attacker is at `to`; remove it, restore at `from`.
445	                        self.board.set(*to, None);
446	                        self.board.set(*from, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(attacker_piece)));
447	                    }
448	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade => {
449	                        // Attacker is gone; restore it at `from`.
450	                        self.board.set(*from, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(attacker_piece)));
451	                    }
452	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe => {
453	                        // Attacker is still at `from`. Nothing to do for it.
454	                    }
455	                }
456	                // Always re-hide the target (it was revealed during apply).
457	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::hidden(revealed_piece)));
458	                // First-flip side assignment may have been set by this move.
459	                if self.history.is_empty() {
460	                    self.side_assignment = None;
461	                }
462	            }
463	        }
464	        Ok(())
465	    }
466	
467	    /// Whether `side`'s general is currently under attack by the opponent.
468	    /// Xiangqi-specific; banqi has no general-in-check concept.
469	    pub fn is_in_check(&self, side: Side) -> bool {
470	        crate::rules::xiangqi::is_in_check(self, side)
471	    }
472	
473	    /// Recompute `status` based on legal moves and draw counters.
474	    /// Caller invokes this after `make_move`; we don't auto-call to avoid
475	    /// recursing into move generation during legality filtering.
476	    ///
477	    /// TODO (PR 2): threefold repetition. Currently only no-progress and
478	    /// no-legal-moves are detected.
479	    pub fn refresh_status(&mut self) {
480	        if matches!(self.status, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) {
481	            return;
482	        }
483	        // Casual xiangqi: a missing general means the opposite side won.
484	        // Standard rules can't reach this branch (the legality filter
485	        // forbids leaving the general capturable), but checking unconditionally
486	        // is cheap and keeps the invariant for any future variant that allows
487	        // physical king capture.
488	        if self.rules.variant == Variant::Xiangqi {
489	            for &seat in self.turn_order.seats.iter() {
490	                let has_general = find_piece(&self.board, |p| {
491	                    p.kind == crate::piece::PieceKind::General && p.side == seat
492	                })
493	                .is_some();
494	                if !has_general {
495	                    self.status = GameStatus::Won {
496	                        winner: seat.opposite(),
497	                        reason: WinReason::GeneralCaptured,
498	                    };
499	                    return;
500	                }
501	            }
502	        }
503	        if self.no_progress_plies >= self.rules.draw_policy.no_progress_plies {
504	            self.status = GameStatus::Drawn { reason: DrawReason::NoProgress };
505	            return;
506	        }
507	        let moves = self.legal_moves();
508	        if moves.is_empty() {
509	            let me = self.side_to_move;
510	            self.status = match self.rules.variant {
511	                Variant::Xiangqi => {
512	                    let reason = if self.is_in_check(me) {
513	                        WinReason::Checkmate
514	                    } else {
515	                        // Asian rules: stalemate is a loss for the stalemated side.
516	                        WinReason::Stalemate
517	                    };
518	                    GameStatus::Won { winner: me.opposite(), reason }
519	                }
520	                Variant::Banqi => GameStatus::Won {
521	                    winner: me.opposite(),
522	                    reason: WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
523	                },
524	                Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {
525	                    // PR 2: more nuanced 3-side win/elimination logic.
526	                    GameStatus::Won {
527	                        winner: me.opposite(),
528	                        reason: WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
529	                    }
530	                }
531	            };
532	        }
533	    }
534	
535	    pub fn default_starter(variant: Variant) -> Side {
536	        match variant {
537	            Variant::Xiangqi => Side::RED,
538	            Variant::Banqi => Side::RED,
539	            Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => Side(0),
540	        }
541	    }
542	
543	    /// The piece-color the active seat actually controls.
544	    ///
545	    /// Banqi remaps after the first flip locks `side_assignment`; xiangqi
546	    /// and three-kingdom always return the seat as-is. Move generation
547	    /// for banqi must filter pieces by this color, not by `side_to_move`
548	    /// — `side_to_move` is the seat / turn-order index, which only
549	    /// coincides with the piece-color before the first reveal.
550	    #[inline]
551	    pub fn current_color(&self) -> Side {
552	        match self.side_assignment.as_ref() {
553	            Some(sa) => {
554	                let idx = self.side_to_move.0 as usize;
555	                if idx < sa.mapping.len() {
556	                    sa.mapping[idx]
557	                } else {
558	                    self.side_to_move
559	                }
560	            }
561	            None => self.side_to_move,
562	        }
563	    }
564	
565	    /// All pieces captured so far, in chronological (history) order.
566	    ///
567	    /// Powers the sidebar "graveyard" panel in both clients. Returns
568	    /// each captured piece's full identity — safe under ADR-0004
569	    /// because every captured piece was revealed at capture time.
570	    /// `Move::DarkCapture` outcomes are recomputed (Probe contributes
571	    /// nothing, Trade contributes the dead attacker, Capture contributes
572	    /// the revealed defender), matching `unmake_move`'s recomputation.
573	    pub fn captured_pieces(&self) -> Vec<Piece> {
574	        let mut out = Vec::new();
575	        for record in &self.history {
576	            match &record.the_move {
577	                Move::Capture { captured, .. } | Move::CannonJump { captured, .. } => {
578	                    out.push(*captured);
579	                }
580	                Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => {
581	                    out.extend(path.iter().map(|hop| hop.captured));
582	                }
583	                Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker } => {
584	                    let (Some(rev), Some(att)) = (*revealed, *attacker) else { continue };
585	                    match dark_capture_outcome(att, rev, self.rules.house, *from, *to, &self.board)
586	                    {
587	                        DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => out.push(rev),
588	                        DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade => out.push(att),
589	                        DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe => {}
590	                    }
591	                }
592	                _ => {}
593	            }
594	        }
595	        out
596	    }
597	}
598	
599	/// Outcome of a 暗吃 (`Move::DarkCapture`) once the target is revealed.
600	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
601	pub(crate) enum DarkCaptureOutcome {
602	    /// Rank check passes — attacker takes target's square (normal capture).
603	    Capture,
604	    /// Rank check fails AND `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` is set — attacker is
605	    /// removed; defender stays revealed at `to`.
606	    Trade,
607	    /// Rank check fails, no trade flag — both pieces stay; target is
608	    /// now revealed (information probe; turn advances normally).
609	    Probe,
610	}
611	
612	/// Decide the outcome of a dark-capture given the involved pieces, the
613	/// displacement (so we can detect rank-bypassing variants), the board
614	/// shape (for the diagonal check), and the active house rules. Used by
615	/// apply, unapply, chain-lock, and graveyard (always recomputed —
616	/// never stored in the move record).
617	///
618	/// Two rank-bypass paths exist:
619	/// * **Cannon attacker** — banqi cannons capture only via jump-over
620	///   screen, and that capture ignores piece rank entirely. Adjacent
621	///   1-step `Move::DarkCapture` is not generated for cannons (see
622	///   `gen_for_face_up_piece`), so any cannon DarkCapture is a
623	///   jump-capture by construction → always `Capture`.
624	/// * **Horse with diagonal displacement under `HORSE_DIAGONAL`** —
625	///   the house rule "馬斜" lets a horse's diagonal *capture* take any
626	///   piece regardless of rank. (Horse orthogonal captures still obey
627	///   rank.)
628	pub(crate) fn dark_capture_outcome(
629	    attacker: Piece,
630	    revealed: Piece,
631	    house: crate::rules::HouseRules,
632	    from: crate::coord::Square,
633	    to: crate::coord::Square,
634	    board: &crate::board::Board,
635	) -> DarkCaptureOutcome {
636	    use crate::piece::PieceKind;
637	    if attacker.side == revealed.side {
638	        // Same side → never a capture; treat as probe (own piece blocks).
639	        return DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe;
640	    }
641	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Cannon {
642	        return DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture;
643	    }
644	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Horse
645	        && house.contains(crate::rules::HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL)
646	        && is_diagonal_step(board, from, to)
647	    {
648	        return DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture;
649	    }
650	    if crate::rules::banqi::can_capture(attacker.kind, revealed.kind) {
651	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture
652	    } else if house.contains(crate::rules::HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE) {
653	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade
654	    } else {
655	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe
656	    }
657	}
658	
659	fn is_diagonal_step(
660	    board: &crate::board::Board,
661	    from: crate::coord::Square,
662	    to: crate::coord::Square,
663	) -> bool {
664	    crate::coord::Direction::DIAGONAL.iter().any(|&d| board.step(from, d) == Some(to))
665	}
666	
667	/// Whether `attacker` standing on `from` has at least one further legal
668	/// capture in any direction. Used to decide whether banqi 連吃 chain
669	/// mode should remain active after a capture (see
670	/// `compute_chain_lock_after`). Only consults banqi rules — caller
671	/// guarantees variant + CHAIN_CAPTURE flag.
672	fn has_capture_from(state: &GameState, from: crate::coord::Square, attacker: Piece) -> bool {
673	    use crate::coord::Direction;
674	    use crate::piece::PieceKind;
675	    use crate::rules::{banqi::can_capture, HouseRules};
676	
677	    let house = state.rules.house;
678	    let board = &state.board;
679	
680	    // Cannon: only via jump-over-screen. Hidden targets past the screen
681	    // count under DARK_CAPTURE — the cannon-jump dark-capture always
682	    // resolves to Capture (rank bypass), so chain mode should
683	    // continue.
684	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Cannon {
685	        for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
686	            let (_walked, screen) = board.ray(from, dir);
687	            let Some(screen_sq) = screen else { continue };
688	            let mut cursor = screen_sq;
689	            while let Some(next) = board.step(cursor, dir) {
690	                match board.get(next) {
691	                    None => cursor = next,
692	                    Some(pos) => {
693	                        if pos.revealed && pos.piece.side != attacker.side {
694	                            return true;
695	                        }
696	                        if !pos.revealed && house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) {
697	                            return true;
698	                        }
699	                        break;
700	                    }
701	                }
702	            }
703	        }
704	        return false;
705	    }
706	
707	    // Orthogonal one-step (and chariot rush extension if enabled).
708	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
709	        let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
710	        match board.get(to) {
711	            None => {}
712	            Some(target) if !target.revealed && house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) => {
713	                return true;
714	            }
715	            Some(target) if !target.revealed => {
716	                // Dark target but DARK_CAPTURE rule disabled: blocked.
717	                let _ = target;
718	            }
719	            Some(target) if target.piece.side == attacker.side => {}
720	            Some(target) if can_capture(attacker.kind, target.piece.kind) => {
721	                return true;
722	            }
723	            Some(_) => {}
724	        }
725	    }
726	
727	    // Chariot rush — multi-square ray; with a gap, captures any piece.
728	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Chariot && house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH) {
729	        for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
730	            let (walked, blocker) = board.ray(from, dir);
731	            if walked.is_empty() {
732	                // Rank-bound 1-step capture is the base-rule branch above.
733	                continue;
734	            }
735	            let Some(target_sq) = blocker else { continue };
736	            let Some(pos) = board.get(target_sq) else { continue };
737	            if pos.revealed && pos.piece.side != attacker.side {
738	                return true;
739	            }
740	            if !pos.revealed && house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) {
741	                return true;
742	            }
743	        }
744	    }
745	
746	    // 馬斜 — diagonal one-step, captures any piece.
747	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Horse && house.contains(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL) {
748	        for &dir in &Direction::DIAGONAL {
749	            let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
750	            match board.get(to) {
751	                None => {}
752	                Some(target) if !target.revealed && house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) => {
753	                    return true;
754	                }
755	                Some(target) if !target.revealed => {
756	                    // Dark target but DARK_CAPTURE rule disabled: blocked.
757	                    let _ = target;
758	                }
759	                Some(target) if target.piece.side == attacker.side => {}
760	                Some(_) => return true,
761	            }
762	        }
763	    }
764	
765	    false
766	}
767	
768	fn banqi_side_assignment(flipper: Side, revealed_side: Side) -> SideAssignment {
769	    let mut mapping = SmallVec::new();
770	    if flipper == Side::RED {
771	        mapping.push(revealed_side);
772	        mapping.push(revealed_side.opposite());
773	    } else {
774	        mapping.push(revealed_side.opposite());
775	        mapping.push(revealed_side);
776	    }
777	    SideAssignment { mapping }
778	}
779	
780	/// Find the (single) piece matching predicate. Used to locate a general.
781	pub(crate) fn find_piece<F>(board: &Board, pred: F) -> Option<crate::coord::Square>
782	where
783	    F: Fn(Piece) -> bool,
784	{
785	    for sq in board.squares() {
786	        if let Some(pos) = board.get(sq) {
787	            if pos.revealed && pred(pos.piece) {
788	                return Some(sq);
789	            }
790	        }
791	    }
792	    None
793	}
794	
795	#[cfg(test)]

822	        state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
