```text
1	//! Move enum.
2	//!
3	//! Flat enum (not a trait) so serde, equality, and pattern matching work
4	//! without ceremony. The `Reveal` variant carries `Option<Piece>` because
5	//! the network ABI distinguishes pre-flip (`None` from client) from
6	//! post-flip (`Some` from authoritative server). See ADR-0004.
7	
8	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
9	use smallvec::SmallVec;
10	
11	use crate::coord::Square;
12	use crate::piece::Piece;
13	
14	/// One logical move from a player's perspective.
15	///
16	/// `ChainCapture` is a single move even though it encodes a sequence —
17	/// undo pops once, history records once, the network ships one message.
18	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
19	pub enum Move {
20	    /// Banqi: flip the face-down piece at `at`. The identity is `None`
21	    /// when the client requests the flip and `Some(_)` once the
22	    /// authoritative engine has resolved it.
23	    Reveal { at: Square, revealed: Option<Piece> },
24	
25	    /// Plain non-capturing slide.
26	    Step { from: Square, to: Square },
27	
28	    /// Capture (single hop). `captured` is recorded so undo can restore.
29	    Capture { from: Square, to: Square, captured: Piece },
30	
31	    /// Chain capture (banqi house rule 連吃 / 暗連). `path` is the sequence
32	    /// of hops, each of which is a capture. Last hop's `to` is the final
33	    /// landing square. Length ≥ 1.
34	    ChainCapture { from: Square, path: SmallVec<[ChainHop; 4]> },
35	
36	    /// Cannon capture-by-jump. `screen` is the piece jumped over.
37	    CannonJump { from: Square, to: Square, screen: Square, captured: Piece },
38	
39	    /// Banqi house rule `暗吃` (DARK_CAPTURE): atomically reveal a
40	    /// face-down piece and resolve a capture against it.
41	    /// `revealed` and `attacker` are `None` on the wire (client → server)
42	    /// and `Some(_)` once the authoritative engine has filled them in.
43	    /// Outcome (capture / probe / trade) is decided at apply-time from
44	    /// banqi rank rules + `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` flag.
45	    DarkCapture { from: Square, to: Square, revealed: Option<Piece>, attacker: Option<Piece> },
46	
47	    /// Banqi 連吃 chain-mode terminator. After a chain-eligible capture,
48	    /// `state.chain_lock` is set and the turn does NOT auto-advance —
49	    /// the player keeps moving the same piece via further captures.
50	    /// `Move::EndChain { at }` is the explicit "I'm done" pass: it clears
51	    /// `chain_lock` and advances the turn. `at` is the locked piece's
52	    /// square (carried so undo can restore).
53	    EndChain { at: Square },
54	}
55	
56	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
57	pub struct ChainHop {
58	    pub to: Square,
59	    pub captured: Piece,
60	}
61	
62	/// Move-list type alias — sized to avoid heap allocation in typical positions.
63	pub type MoveList = SmallVec<[Move; 32]>;
64	
65	impl Move {
66	    /// Origin square of the move (where the moving piece started).
67	    /// `Reveal` returns the flip square. `EndChain` returns the locked
68	    /// piece's square (the piece doesn't move).
69	    #[inline]
70	    pub fn origin_square(&self) -> Square {
71	        match self {
72	            Move::Reveal { at, .. } => *at,
73	            Move::EndChain { at } => *at,
74	            Move::Step { from, .. }
75	            | Move::Capture { from, .. }
76	            | Move::ChainCapture { from, .. }
77	            | Move::CannonJump { from, .. }
78	            | Move::DarkCapture { from, .. } => *from,
79	        }
80	    }
81	
82	    /// Destination square (where the moving piece ended). `Reveal` and
83	    /// `EndChain` have none (the piece doesn't move).
84	    #[inline]
85	    pub fn to_square(&self) -> Option<Square> {
86	        match self {
87	            Move::Reveal { .. } | Move::EndChain { .. } => None,
88	            Move::Step { to, .. }
89	            | Move::Capture { to, .. }
90	            | Move::CannonJump { to, .. }
91	            | Move::DarkCapture { to, .. } => Some(*to),
92	            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => path.last().map(|h| h.to),
93	        }
94	    }
95	
96	    /// Whether this move resets the no-progress counter (capture or reveal).
97	    /// `EndChain` is a turn pass — it does NOT reset no_progress.
98	    #[inline]
99	    pub fn resets_no_progress(&self) -> bool {
100	        matches!(
101	            self,
102	            Move::Reveal { .. }
103	                | Move::Capture { .. }
104	                | Move::ChainCapture { .. }
105	                | Move::CannonJump { .. }
106	                | Move::DarkCapture { .. }
107	        )
108	    }
109	}
110
```

```text
1	//! Board: dense storage + shape-aware coordinate operations.
2	
3	pub mod grid;
4	pub mod shape;
5	pub mod zones;
6	
7	pub use shape::BoardShape;
8	pub use zones::RegionKind;
9	
10	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
11	use smallvec::SmallVec;
12	
13	use crate::coord::{Direction, File, Rank, Square};
14	use crate::piece::PieceOnSquare;
15	
16	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
17	pub struct Board {
18	    shape: BoardShape,
19	    width: u8,
20	    height: u8,
21	    cells: Vec<Option<PieceOnSquare>>,
22	}
23	
24	impl Board {
25	    pub fn new(shape: BoardShape) -> Self {
26	        let (width, height) = shape.dimensions();
27	        Self { shape, width, height, cells: vec![None; shape.cell_count()] }
28	    }
29	
30	    #[inline]
31	    pub fn shape(&self) -> BoardShape {
32	        self.shape
33	    }
34	
35	    #[inline]
36	    pub fn width(&self) -> u8 {
37	        self.width
38	    }
39	
40	    #[inline]
41	    pub fn height(&self) -> u8 {
42	        self.height
43	    }
44	
45	    #[inline]
46	    pub fn cell_count(&self) -> usize {
47	        self.cells.len()
48	    }
49	
50	    /// Build a `Square` from `(file, rank)` on this board.
51	    #[inline]
52	    pub fn sq(&self, file: File, rank: Rank) -> Square {
53	        Square((rank.0 as u16) * (self.width as u16) + file.0 as u16)
54	    }
55	
56	    /// Decompose a `Square` into `(file, rank)`.
57	    #[inline]
58	    pub fn file_rank(&self, sq: Square) -> (File, Rank) {
59	        let w = self.width as u16;
60	        (File((sq.0 % w) as u8), Rank((sq.0 / w) as u8))
61	    }
62	
63	    pub fn get(&self, sq: Square) -> Option<PieceOnSquare> {
64	        self.cells.get(sq.0 as usize).copied().flatten()
65	    }
66	
67	    pub fn set(&mut self, sq: Square, value: Option<PieceOnSquare>) {
68	        self.cells[sq.0 as usize] = value;
69	    }
70	
71	    /// Iterate every playable square in linear order.
72	    pub fn squares(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = Square> + '_ {
73	        grid::squares_of(self.shape)
74	    }
75	
76	    /// One step in `dir` from `from`. Returns `None` if the destination
77	    /// would leave the board or land on a non-playable cell.
78	    pub fn step(&self, from: Square, dir: Direction) -> Option<Square> {
79	        let (df, dr) = dir.delta();
80	        let (file, rank) = self.file_rank(from);
81	        let nf = (file.0 as i16) + df as i16;
82	        let nr = (rank.0 as i16) + dr as i16;
83	        if nf < 0 || nr < 0 || nf >= self.width as i16 || nr >= self.height as i16 {
84	            return None;
85	        }
86	        let next = self.sq(File(nf as u8), Rank(nr as u8));
87	        if self.shape.is_playable(next.0 as usize) {
88	            Some(next)
89	        } else {
90	            None
91	        }
92	    }
93	
94	    /// Cast a ray from `from` in `dir`. Returns the squares walked over
95	    /// (all empty) and, if the ray stopped on an occupied square, that
96	    /// square. Stops at the board edge with `None` blocker.
97	    pub fn ray(&self, from: Square, dir: Direction) -> (SmallVec<[Square; 16]>, Option<Square>) {
98	        let mut walked = SmallVec::new();
99	        let mut cursor = from;
100	        loop {
101	            match self.step(cursor, dir) {
102	                None => return (walked, None),
103	                Some(next) => {
104	                    if self.get(next).is_some() {
105	                        return (walked, Some(next));
106	                    }
107	                    walked.push(next);
108	                    cursor = next;
109	                }
110	            }
111	        }
112	    }
113	
114	    /// Convenience predicate forwarding to [`zones::in_region`].
115	    #[inline]
116	    pub fn in_region(&self, sq: Square, region: RegionKind) -> bool {
117	        zones::in_region(self.shape, sq, region)
118	    }
119	}
120
```

```text
1	//! Variant-slug parsing for `/local/:variant` plus URL-query rule encoding.
2	//!
3	//! Pure logic — native-testable. The picker page builds a query string from
4	//! its form state via [`build_local_query`]; the local page parses it back
5	//! into a [`RuleSet`] via [`parse_local_rules`] + [`build_rule_set`].
6	
7	use chess_core::rules::{
8	    house, HouseRules, RuleSet, Variant, PRESET_AGGRESSIVE, PRESET_PURIST, PRESET_TAIWAN,
9	};
10	
11	pub fn hosting_mode() -> &'static str {
12	    env!("CHESS_WEB_HOSTING")
13	}
14	
15	pub fn is_static_hosting() -> bool {
16	    hosting_mode() == "static"
17	}
18	
19	pub fn base_path() -> &'static str {
20	    env!("CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH")
21	}
22	
23	pub fn router_base() -> Option<&'static str> {
24	    let base = base_path();
25	    if base.is_empty() {
26	        None
27	    } else {
28	        Some(base)
29	    }
30	}
31	
32	pub fn app_href(path: &str) -> String {
33	    app_href_with_base(base_path(), path)
34	}
35	
36	pub fn app_href_with_base(base: &str, path: &str) -> String {
37	    let path = if path.is_empty() { "/" } else { path };
38	    let path = if path.starts_with('/') { path.to_string() } else { format!("/{path}") };
39	    let base = normalize_base_path(base);
40	    if base.is_empty() {
41	        path
42	    } else if path == "/" {
43	        format!("{base}/")
44	    } else {
45	        format!("{base}{path}")
46	    }
47	}
48	
49	fn normalize_base_path(base: &str) -> String {
50	    let trimmed = base.trim().trim_matches('/');
51	    if trimmed.is_empty() {
52	        String::new()
53	    } else {
54	        format!("/{trimmed}")
55	    }
56	}
57	
58	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
59	pub enum WsBaseError {
60	    Empty,
61	    BadScheme,
62	}
63	
64	pub fn normalize_ws_base(raw: &str) -> Result<String, WsBaseError> {
65	    let trimmed = raw.trim().trim_end_matches('/');
66	    if trimmed.is_empty() {
67	        return Err(WsBaseError::Empty);
68	    }
69	    if !(trimmed.starts_with("ws://") || trimmed.starts_with("wss://")) {
70	        return Err(WsBaseError::BadScheme);
71	    }
72	    Ok(trimmed.to_string())
73	}
74	
75	pub fn parse_variant_slug(slug: &str) -> Option<Variant> {
76	    match slug {
77	        "xiangqi" => Some(Variant::Xiangqi),
78	        "banqi" => Some(Variant::Banqi),
79	        "three-kingdom" => Some(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi),
80	        _ => None,
81	    }
82	}
83	
84	pub fn variant_slug(variant: Variant) -> &'static str {
85	    match variant {
86	        Variant::Xiangqi => "xiangqi",
87	        Variant::Banqi => "banqi",
88	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "three-kingdom",
89	    }
90	}
91	
92	/// URL-derived rule choices for `/local/:variant`. Used as an intermediate
93	/// between the picker form and the engine `RuleSet`.
94	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Default)]
95	pub struct LocalRulesParams {
96	    /// Xiangqi only. `false` (default) = casual / no self-check filter;
97	    /// `true` = standard rules where leaving your general in check is illegal.
98	    pub strict: bool,
99	    /// Banqi only. Bitflag set; `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` auto-implies
100	    /// `DARK_CAPTURE` after [`house::normalize`] in the parser.
101	    pub house: HouseRules,
102	    /// Banqi only. `None` = engine picks (non-deterministic on native;
103	    /// browser uses `getrandom` JS feature).
104	    pub seed: Option<u64>,
105	}
106	
107	/// Parse `?strict=1&house=chain,rush&preset=taiwan&seed=42` into a
108	/// [`LocalRulesParams`]. Unknown tokens are silently dropped — this is a
109	/// best-effort decoder for shareable URLs.
110	///
111	/// `house=` overrides `preset=` when both are present; the picker only
112	/// emits `house=` so this just covers manually-typed URLs.
113	pub fn parse_local_rules(get: impl Fn(&str) -> Option<String>) -> LocalRulesParams {
114	    let strict = matches!(get("strict").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true"));
115	    let preset = get("preset").as_deref().and_then(parse_preset_token);
116	    let house_csv = get("house").as_deref().map(parse_house_csv);
117	    let house = house_csv.or(preset).unwrap_or_else(HouseRules::empty);
118	    let seed = get("seed").and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());
119	    LocalRulesParams { strict, house: house::normalize(house), seed }
120	}
121	
122	/// Inverse of [`parse_local_rules`] — emits a stable canonical query string
123	/// (no leading `?`). Empty result means "use defaults" — the picker drops the
124	/// `?` entirely so `/local/xiangqi` with no query is the default-casual URL.
125	pub fn build_local_query(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
126	    let mut parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
127	    match variant {
128	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
129	            if params.strict {
130	                parts.push("strict=1".to_string());
131	            }
132	        }
133	        Variant::Banqi => {
134	            let csv = house_csv(params.house);
135	            if !csv.is_empty() {
136	                parts.push(format!("house={}", csv));
137	            }
138	            if let Some(seed) = params.seed {
139	                parts.push(format!("seed={}", seed));
140	            }
141	        }
142	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {}
143	    }
144	    parts.join("&")
145	}
146	
147	/// Build the full path for the picker's "Start" link given the current form.
148	/// Always returns `"/local/<slug>"` with the query string appended only when
149	/// non-empty.
150	pub fn build_local_href(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
151	    let q = build_local_query(variant, params);
152	    if q.is_empty() {
153	        format!("/local/{}", variant_slug(variant))
154	    } else {
155	        format!("/local/{}?{}", variant_slug(variant), q)
156	    }
157	}
158	
159	/// Convert parsed [`LocalRulesParams`] into the engine `RuleSet` for the
160	/// chosen variant. The picker → URL → page flow is the only call site.
161	pub fn build_rule_set(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> RuleSet {
162	    match variant {
163	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
164	            if params.strict {
165	                RuleSet::xiangqi()
166	            } else {
167	                RuleSet::xiangqi_casual()
168	            }
169	        }
170	        Variant::Banqi => match params.seed {
171	            Some(seed) => RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(params.house, seed),
172	            None => RuleSet::banqi(params.house),
173	        },
174	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => RuleSet::three_kingdom(),
175	    }
176	}
177	
178	fn parse_house_csv(s: &str) -> HouseRules {
179	    let mut out = HouseRules::empty();
180	    for tok in s.split(',').map(str::trim).filter(|t| !t.is_empty()) {
181	        if let Some(flag) = parse_house_token(tok) {
182	            out.insert(flag);
183	        }
184	    }
185	    out
186	}
187	
188	fn house_csv(rules: HouseRules) -> String {
189	    let mut parts: Vec<&'static str> = Vec::new();
190	    for (flag, tok) in HOUSE_TOKENS {
191	        if rules.contains(*flag) {
192	            parts.push(tok);
193	        }
194	    }
195	    parts.join(",")
196	}
197	
198	fn parse_house_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
199	    HOUSE_TOKENS
200	        .iter()
201	        .chain(HOUSE_TOKEN_ALIASES.iter())
202	        .find(|(_, t)| t.eq_ignore_ascii_case(tok))
203	        .map(|(f, _)| *f)
204	}
205	
206	fn parse_preset_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
207	    match tok.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
208	        "purist" => Some(PRESET_PURIST),
209	        "taiwan" => Some(PRESET_TAIWAN),
210	        "aggressive" => Some(PRESET_AGGRESSIVE),
211	        _ => None,
212	    }
213	}
214	
215	const HOUSE_TOKENS: &[(HouseRules, &str)] = &[
216	    (HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, "chain"),
217	    (HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, "dark"),
218	    (HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH, "rush"),
219	    (HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, "horse"),
220	    (HouseRules::CANNON_FAST_MOVE, "cannon"),
221	    (HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE, "dark-trade"),
222	];
223	
224	/// Aliases recognised on parse only (older URLs / pre-rename snapshots).
225	/// Encoding uses the canonical `HOUSE_TOKENS` only.
226	const HOUSE_TOKEN_ALIASES: &[(HouseRules, &str)] = &[(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, "dark-chain")];
227

231	
232	    fn from_pairs<'a>(pairs: &'a [(&'a str, &'a str)]) -> impl Fn(&str) -> Option<String> + 'a {
233	        move |k: &str| pairs.iter().find(|(pk, _)| *pk == k).map(|(_, v)| v.to_string())
234	    }
235	
236	    #[test]
237	    fn known_slugs_parse() {
238	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("xiangqi"), Some(Variant::Xiangqi)));
239	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("banqi"), Some(Variant::Banqi)));
240	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("three-kingdom"), Some(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi)));
241	    }
242	
243	    #[test]
244	    fn unknown_slug_returns_none() {
245	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("chess").is_none());
246	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("").is_none());
247	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("XIANGQI").is_none());
248	    }
249	
250	    #[test]
251	    fn slug_round_trip() {
252	        for v in [Variant::Xiangqi, Variant::Banqi, Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi] {
253	            assert_eq!(parse_variant_slug(variant_slug(v)), Some(v));
254	        }

256	
257	    #[test]

259	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[]));
```

```text
1	use chess_core::coord::Square;
2	use chess_core::piece::Side;
3	use chess_core::rules::{RuleSet, Variant};
4	use chess_core::state::GameState;
5	use chess_core::view::{PlayerView, VisibleCell};
6	use leptos::*;
7	use leptos_router::{use_params_map, use_query_map};
8	
9	use crate::components::board::Board;
10	use crate::components::captured::CapturedStrip;
11	use crate::components::end_overlay::EndOverlay;
12	use crate::components::sidebar::Sidebar;
13	use crate::prefs::Prefs;
14	use crate::routes::{app_href, build_rule_set, parse_local_rules, parse_variant_slug};
15	use crate::state::{end_chain_move, find_move};
16	
17	#[component]
18	pub fn LocalPage() -> impl IntoView {
19	    let params = use_params_map();
20	    let query = use_query_map();
21	    let resolved = move || -> Result<(Variant, RuleSet, bool), String> {
22	        let slug = params.with(|p| p.get("variant").cloned().unwrap_or_default());
23	        let variant =
24	            parse_variant_slug(&slug).ok_or_else(|| format!("Unknown variant: {}", slug))?;
25	        let parsed = query.with(|q| parse_local_rules(|k| q.get(k).cloned()));
26	        let rules = build_rule_set(variant, &parsed);
27	        // Three-kingdom: engine ships an empty 4×8 board. Render it but
28	        // disable interaction and overlay a "WIP" banner — see
29	        // `backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md` and the gotcha in CLAUDE.md.
30	        let wip = matches!(variant, Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi);
31	        Ok((variant, rules, wip))
32	    };
33	
34	    move || match resolved() {
35	        Ok((variant, rules, wip)) => {
36	            view! { <LocalGame variant=variant rules=rules wip=wip/> }.into_view()
37	        }
38	        Err(msg) => view! {
39	            <section class="game-page game-page--single">
40	                <div>
41	                    <a href=app_href("/") rel="external" class="back-link">"← Back to picker"</a>
42	                    <h2>"Variant unavailable"</h2>
43	                    <p class="subtitle">{msg}</p>
44	                </div>
45	            </section>
46	        }
47	        .into_view(),
48	    }
49	}
50	
51	#[component]
52	fn LocalGame(variant: Variant, rules: RuleSet, wip: bool) -> impl IntoView {
53	    let initial_rules = rules.clone();
54	    let state = create_rw_signal(GameState::new(rules));
55	    let selected = create_rw_signal::<Option<Square>>(None);
56	
57	    // Local pass-and-play renders from a fixed Red-side observer (board never
58	    // flips on turn change). Legal moves come from the projection for the
59	    // *current* side-to-move so click-to-move uses the right list.
60	    let observer = Side::RED;
61	    let shape = state.with_untracked(|s| s.board.shape());
62	
63	    let view = create_memo(move |_| state.with(|s| PlayerView::project(s, s.side_to_move)));
64	
65	    // Clear any held selection when the seat-to-move flips. Catches the
66	    // chain-mode tail where the last hop ends the chain and switches
67	    // sides — the previous player's `selected` is meaningless for the
68	    // next one. Also a safety net for any future click path that
69	    // forgets its own `selected.set(None)`.
70	    let side_signal = Signal::derive(move || view.with(|v| v.side_to_move));
71	    create_effect(move |prev: Option<Side>| {
72	        let cur = side_signal.get();
73	        if prev.is_some_and(|p| p != cur) {
74	            selected.set(None);
75	        }
76	        cur
77	    });
78	
79	    // The chain-locked piece (if any) renders with the same affordance as
80	    // a manually-selected piece, so the user sees it visually highlighted.
81	    let chain_locked_signal: Signal<Option<Square>> =
82	        Signal::derive(move || view.with(|v| v.chain_lock));
83	
84	    let effective_selected: Signal<Option<Square>> = Signal::derive(move || {
85	        // In chain mode the engine dictates which piece moves; surface
86	        // chain_lock as the selected square so the legal-target dots
87	        // render around it.
88	        chain_locked_signal.get().or_else(|| selected.get())
89	    });
90	
91	    let on_end_chain: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_| {
92	        if wip {
93	            return;
94	        }
95	        let v = view.get();
96	        if let Some(mv) = end_chain_move(&v) {
97	            state.update(|s| {
98	                s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal end-chain");
99	                s.refresh_status();
100	            });
101	            selected.set(None);
102	        }
103	    });
104	
105	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
106	        if wip {
107	            return;
108	        }
109	        let v = view.get();
110	
111	        // Chain mode is engine-driven: when chain_lock is set, only the
112	        // locked piece can move (captures only). Clicking the locked
113	        // piece itself is the explicit "end chain" gesture.
114	        if let Some(locked) = v.chain_lock {
115	            if sq == locked {
116	                if let Some(mv) = end_chain_move(&v) {
117	                    state.update(|s| {
118	                        s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal end-chain");
119	                        s.refresh_status();
120	                    });
121	                }
122	                selected.set(None);
123	                return;
124	            }
125	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, locked, sq) {
126	                state.update(|s| {
127	                    s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal chain capture");
128	                    s.refresh_status();
129	                });
130	                selected.set(None);
131	                return;
132	            }
133	            // Click somewhere else during chain mode: ignore (the user
134	            // must end the chain or capture). Selection is irrelevant.
135	            return;
136	        }
137	
138	        let cur = selected.get();
139	
140	        if cur == Some(sq) {
141	            selected.set(None);
142	            return;
143	        }
144	
145	        if let Some(from) = cur {
146	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, from, sq) {
147	                state.update(|s| {
148	                    s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal move from view");
149	                    s.refresh_status();
150	                });
151	                selected.set(None);
152	                return;
153	            }
154	        }
155	
156	        let cell = v.cells[sq.0 as usize];
157	        // Selectable iff the engine emits a legal move from this square — this
158	        // works regardless of `side_assignment` remapping (banqi after the
159	        // first flip), since `legal_moves` is already filtered by piece-color.
160	        let is_selectable_revealed = matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Revealed(_))
161	            && v.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq);
162	        if is_selectable_revealed {
163	            selected.set(Some(sq));
164	        } else if matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Hidden) {
165	            // Banqi flip — engine emits `Reveal { at }` whose origin and
166	            // target both equal `sq`. Apply directly.
167	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, sq, sq) {
168	                state.update(|s| {
169	                    s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal reveal");
170	                    s.refresh_status();
171	                });
172	                selected.set(None);
173	            }
174	        } else {
175	            selected.set(None);
176	        }
177	    });
178	
179	    let on_undo: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_| {
180	        if wip {
181	            return;
182	        }
183	        state.update(|s| {
184	            let _ = s.unmake_move();
185	            s.refresh_status();
186	        });
187	        selected.set(None);
188	    });
189	
190	    let on_new_game: Callback<()> = Callback::new({
191	        let initial_rules = initial_rules.clone();
192	        move |_| {
193	            state.set(GameState::new(initial_rules.clone()));
194	            selected.set(None);
195	        }
196	    });
197	
198	    let prefs = expect_context::<Prefs>();
199	    let fx_confetti: Signal<bool> = prefs.fx_confetti.into();
200	    // Local pass-and-play: no `ClientRole`, so the overlay falls back to
201	    // the neutral "Red Wins" / "Black Wins" framing.
202	    let role_signal: Signal<Option<crate::state::ClientRole>> = Signal::derive(|| None);
203	
204	    let chain_active = Signal::derive(move || chain_locked_signal.get().is_some());
205	
206	    view! {
207	        <section class="game-page">
208	            <div class="board-pane">
209	                <Board
210	                    shape=shape
211	                    observer=observer
212	                    view=view
213	                    selected=effective_selected
214	                    on_click=on_click
215	                />
216	                <Show when=move || chain_active.get()>
217	                    <div class="chain-banner">
218	                        <span>"連吃 — 繼續吃 or "</span>
219	                        <button class="btn btn-ghost btn-sm" on:click=move |_| on_end_chain.call(())>
220	                            "End chain"
221	                        </button>
222	                    </div>
223	                </Show>
224	                <Show when=move || wip>
225	                    <div class="wip-overlay">
226	                        <div class="wip-banner">
227	                            <h3>"Three-Kingdom Banqi 三國暗棋"</h3>
228	                            <p>
229	                                "Engine still WIP — pieces, rules, and 3-seat turn order land in PR-2. "
230	                                "See "
231	                                <code>"backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md"</code>
232	                                "."
233	                            </p>
234	                        </div>
235	                    </div>
236	                </Show>
237	                <Show when=move || !wip>
238	                    <EndOverlay view=view role=role_signal enabled=fx_confetti/>
239	                </Show>
240	                <Show when=move || !wip>
241	                    <CapturedStrip view=view/>
242	                </Show>
243	            </div>
244	            <Sidebar
245	                variant=variant
246	                view=view
247	                on_new_game=on_new_game
248	                on_undo=on_undo
249	                wip=wip
250	            />
251	        </section>
252	    }
253	}
254
```

 `./clients/chess-web/src/components/board.rs`

```text
1	//! SVG board renderer. Variant-agnostic: takes a `PlayerView` + observer +
2	//! a click callback, and lays cells on the intersection grid that the TUI
3	//! also uses (see `crate::orient`).
4	
5	use chess_core::board::BoardShape;
6	use chess_core::coord::Square;
7	use chess_core::piece::Side;
8	use chess_core::view::{PlayerView, VisibleCell};
9	use leptos::*;
10	
11	use crate::glyph::{self, Style};
12	use crate::orient::{display_dims, square_at_display};
13	use crate::state::legal_targets;
14	
15	pub const CELL: i32 = 60;
16	pub const PAD: i32 = 30;
17	const RIVER_TOP_ROW: u8 = 4;
18	const RIVER_BOT_ROW: u8 = 5;
19	const TILE_RADIUS: i32 = 24;
20	
21	#[component]
22	pub fn Board(
23	    shape: BoardShape,
24	    observer: Side,
25	    #[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>,
26	    #[prop(into)] selected: Signal<Option<Square>>,
27	    #[prop(into)] on_click: Callback<Square>,
28	) -> impl IntoView {
29	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
30	    let view_w = (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL + PAD * 2;
31	    let view_h = (rows as i32 - 1) * CELL + PAD * 2;
32	    let viewbox = format!("0 0 {} {}", view_w, view_h);
33	    let style = Style::Cjk;
34	
35	    view! {
36	        <svg class="board" viewBox=viewbox preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet">
37	            <rect class="board-bg" x="0" y="0" width="100%" height="100%"/>
38	            <g class="grid-layer">{grid_lines(shape, rows, cols)}</g>
39	            <g class="river-layer">{river_text(shape, cols)}</g>
40	            <g class="palace-layer">{palace_diagonals(shape)}</g>
41	            <g class="overlay-layer">
42	                {move || move_dots_view(&view.get(), selected.get(), observer, shape)}
43	            </g>
44	            <g class="pieces-layer">
45	                {move || pieces_view(&view.get(), observer, shape, style)}
46	            </g>
47	            <g class="overlay-top-layer">
48	                {move || chain_lock_marker(view.get().chain_lock, observer, shape)}
49	                {move || selection_marker(selected.get(), observer, shape)}
50	            </g>
51	            <g class="cells-layer">
52	                {hit_cells(rows, cols, observer, shape, on_click)}
53	            </g>
54	        </svg>
55	    }
56	}
57	
58	#[inline]
59	fn intersection(row: u8, col: u8) -> (i32, i32) {
60	    (PAD + col as i32 * CELL, PAD + row as i32 * CELL)
61	}
62	
63	fn grid_lines(shape: BoardShape, rows: u8, cols: u8) -> View {
64	    let is_xiangqi = matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10);
65	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::with_capacity(rows as usize + cols as usize * 2);
66	    for r in 0..rows {
67	        let y = PAD + r as i32 * CELL;
68	        let x1 = PAD;
69	        let x2 = PAD + (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL;
70	        out.push(view! { <line class="grid" x1=x1 y1=y x2=x2 y2=y/> }.into_view());
71	    }
72	    for c in 0..cols {
73	        let x = PAD + c as i32 * CELL;
74	        let is_border = c == 0 || c == cols - 1;
75	        let y_top = PAD;
76	        let y_bot = PAD + (rows as i32 - 1) * CELL;
77	        if is_xiangqi && !is_border {
78	            let y_river_top = PAD + RIVER_TOP_ROW as i32 * CELL;
79	            let y_river_bot = PAD + RIVER_BOT_ROW as i32 * CELL;
80	            out.push(
81	                view! {
82	                    <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_top x2=x y2=y_river_top/>
83	                    <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_river_bot x2=x y2=y_bot/>
84	                }
85	                .into_view(),
86	            );
87	        } else {
88	            out.push(view! { <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_top x2=x y2=y_bot/> }.into_view());
89	        }
90	    }
91	    out.into_view()
92	}
93	
94	fn river_text(shape: BoardShape, cols: u8) -> View {
95	    if !matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) {
96	        return ().into_view();
97	    }
98	    let cx = PAD + (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL / 2;
99	    let cy = PAD + (RIVER_TOP_ROW as i32 * CELL + RIVER_BOT_ROW as i32 * CELL) / 2;
100	    view! {
101	        <text class="river-text" x=cx y=cy text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">
102	            "楚 河 漢 界"
103	        </text>
104	    }
105	    .into_view()
106	}
107	
108	fn palace_diagonals(shape: BoardShape) -> View {
109	    if !matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) {
110	        return ().into_view();
111	    }
112	    // Files 3..5; top palace = display rows 0..2; bottom palace = rows 7..9.
113	    // Symmetric for both observers because the palace box is centered on the file axis.
114	    let segments = [
115	        (intersection(0, 3), intersection(2, 5)),
116	        (intersection(0, 5), intersection(2, 3)),
117	        (intersection(7, 3), intersection(9, 5)),
118	        (intersection(7, 5), intersection(9, 3)),
119	    ];
120	    segments
121	        .into_iter()
122	        .map(|(a, b)| view! { <line class="palace" x1=a.0 y1=a.1 x2=b.0 y2=b.1/> }.into_view())
123	        .collect::<Vec<View>>()
124	        .into_view()
125	}
126	
127	fn pieces_view(view: &PlayerView, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape, style: Style) -> View {
128	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
129	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::new();
130	    for row in 0..rows {
131	        for col in 0..cols {
132	            let Some(sq) = square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) else { continue };
133	            let cell = view.cells[sq.0 as usize];
134	            let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
135	            match cell {
136	                VisibleCell::Empty => {}
137	                VisibleCell::Hidden => {
138	                    out.push(
139	                        view! {
140	                            <g class="tile hidden" transform=format!("translate({}, {})", cx, cy)>
141	                                <circle class="tile-back" r=TILE_RADIUS cx="0" cy="0"/>
142	                            </g>
143	                        }
144	                        .into_view(),
145	                    );
146	                }
147	                VisibleCell::Revealed(p) => {
148	                    let side_class = match p.piece.side {
149	                        Side::RED => "red",
150	                        Side::BLACK => "black",
151	                        _ => "green",
152	                    };
153	                    out.push(
154	                        view! {
155	                            <g class=format!("tile piece {}", side_class) transform=format!("translate({}, {})", cx, cy)>
156	                                <circle class="tile-disc" r=TILE_RADIUS cx="0" cy="0"/>
157	                                <text class="tile-glyph" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">
158	                                    {glyph::glyph(p.piece.kind, p.piece.side, style)}
159	                                </text>
160	                            </g>
161	                        }
162	                        .into_view(),
163	                    );
164	                }
165	            }
166	        }
167	    }
168	    out.into_view()
169	}
170	
171	fn selection_marker(selected: Option<Square>, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape) -> View {
172	    let Some(sq) = selected else { return ().into_view() };
173	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
174	    for row in 0..rows {
175	        for col in 0..cols {
176	            if square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) == Some(sq) {
177	                let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
178	                return view! { <circle class="selection" cx=cx cy=cy r="28"/> }.into_view();
179	            }
180	        }
181	    }
182	    ().into_view()
183	}
184	
185	fn chain_lock_marker(locked: Option<Square>, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape) -> View {
186	    let Some(sq) = locked else { return ().into_view() };
187	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
188	    for row in 0..rows {
189	        for col in 0..cols {
190	            if square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) == Some(sq) {
191	                let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
192	                return view! { <circle class="chain-lock" cx=cx cy=cy r="30"/> }.into_view();
193	            }
194	        }
195	    }
196	    ().into_view()
197	}
198	
199	fn move_dots_view(
200	    view: &PlayerView,
201	    selected: Option<Square>,
202	    observer: Side,
203	    shape: BoardShape,
204	) -> View {
205	    let Some(from) = selected else { return ().into_view() };
206	    let targets = legal_targets(view, from);
207	    if targets.is_empty() {
208	        return ().into_view();
209	    }
210	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
211	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::new();
212	    for row in 0..rows {
213	        for col in 0..cols {
214	            let Some(sq) = square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) else { continue };
215	            if !targets.contains(&sq) {
216	                continue;
217	            }
218	            let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
219	            // Empty target → small dot. Capturable target (revealed
220	            // enemy piece OR face-down tile under DARK_CAPTURE) →
221	            // larger ring framing the piece, so the player sees at a
222	            // glance which enemies / hidden tiles can be taken — not
223	            // just where to slide to. Same data the TUI surfaces with
224	            // its green-highlighted attackable squares.
225	            let cell = view.cells[sq.0 as usize];
226	            let class = match cell {
227	                VisibleCell::Empty => "move-dot",
228	                _ => "move-target",
229	            };
230	            let r = if class == "move-dot" { "9" } else { "26" };
231	            out.push(view! { <circle class=class cx=cx cy=cy r=r/> }.into_view());
232	        }
233	    }
234	    out.into_view()
235	}
236	
237	fn hit_cells(
238	    rows: u8,
239	    cols: u8,
240	    observer: Side,
241	    shape: BoardShape,
242	    on_click: Callback<Square>,
243	) -> View {
244	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::with_capacity(rows as usize * cols as usize);
245	    for row in 0..rows {
246	        for col in 0..cols {
247	            let Some(sq) = square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) else { continue };
248	            let x = col as i32 * CELL;
249	            let y = row as i32 * CELL;
250	            out.push(
251	                view! {
252	                    <rect
253	                        class="cell-hit"
254	                        x=x y=y width=CELL height=CELL
255	                        on:pointerup=move |_| on_click.call(sq)
256	                    />
257	                }
258	                .into_view(),
259	            );
260	        }
261	    }
262	    out.into_view()
263	}
264
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	//! Sidebar: variant + turn + status + history-length + undo / new-game buttons.
2	//!
3	//! `wip=true` (three-kingdom banqi until the engine ships) hides turn /
4	//! legal-move counts and disables the action buttons — the board overlay
5	//! tells the user what's happening; the sidebar just stays out of the way.
6	
7	use chess_core::board::BoardShape;
8	use chess_core::piece::Side;
9	use chess_core::rules::Variant;
10	use chess_core::state::{DrawReason, GameStatus, WinReason};
11	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
12	use leptos::*;
13	
14	use crate::glyph::{self, Style};
15	use crate::prefs::Prefs;
16	use crate::routes::app_href;
17	
18	#[component]
19	pub fn Sidebar(
20	    variant: Variant,
21	    #[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>,
22	    #[prop(into)] on_new_game: Callback<()>,
23	    #[prop(into)] on_undo: Callback<()>,
24	    #[prop(default = false)] wip: bool,
25	) -> impl IntoView {
26	    let style = Style::Cjk;
27	    let variant_label = match variant {
28	        Variant::Xiangqi => "Xiangqi 象棋",
29	        Variant::Banqi => "Banqi 暗棋",
30	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "Three-Kingdom 三國暗棋",
31	    };
32	
33	    // Display the piece-colour to move, not the seat name. After a banqi
34	    // first-flip the seat (`side_to_move`) and the colour the seat plays
35	    // (`current_color`) diverge — players think in terms of colour.
36	    let turn_text = move || {
37	        let v = view.get();
38	        glyph::side_name(v.current_color, style).to_string()
39	    };
40	    let turn_class = move || {
41	        let v = view.get();
42	        match v.current_color {
43	            Side::RED => "turn-red",
44	            Side::BLACK => "turn-black",
45	            _ => "turn-green",
46	        }
47	    };
48	
49	    let status_view = move || -> View {
50	        let v = view.get();
51	        match v.status {
52	            GameStatus::Ongoing => view! { <p class="status ongoing">"Ongoing"</p> }.into_view(),
53	            GameStatus::Won { winner, reason } => {
54	                let side = glyph::side_name(winner, style);
55	                let reason = win_reason_label(reason);
56	                view! {
57	                    <p class="status won">
58	                        {format!("{} wins — {}", side, reason)}
59	                    </p>
60	                }
61	                .into_view()
62	            }
63	            GameStatus::Drawn { reason } => view! {
64	                <p class="status drawn">
65	                    {format!("Drawn — {}", draw_reason_label(reason))}
66	                </p>
67	            }
68	            .into_view(),
69	        }
70	    };
71	
72	    let legal_count = move || view.with(|v| v.legal_moves.len());
73	
74	    // Pull FX prefs from context (provided by `app.rs::App`). Both signals
75	    // default to true on first visit and persist via localStorage.
76	    let prefs = expect_context::<Prefs>();
77	    let fx_confetti = prefs.fx_confetti;
78	    let fx_check_banner = prefs.fx_check_banner;
79	
80	    let show_check = move || {
81	        let v = view.get();
82	        // Check banner is xiangqi-only and gated by the user pref.
83	        fx_check_banner.get()
84	            && matches!(v.shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10)
85	            && v.in_check
86	            && matches!(v.status, GameStatus::Ongoing)
87	    };
88	
89	    view! {
90	        <aside class="sidebar">
91	            <h3 class="variant-label">{variant_label}</h3>
92	            <Show when=move || !wip fallback=|| view! {
93	                <p class="status drawn">"Engine WIP — board interaction disabled."</p>
94	            }>
95	                <Show when=show_check>
96	                    <div class="check-badge" role="status">"⚠ 將軍 / CHECK"</div>
97	                </Show>
98	                <div class="turn-row">
99	                    <span class="turn-prefix">"Turn: "</span>
100	                    <span class=turn_class>{turn_text}</span>
101	                </div>
102	                {status_view}
103	                <p class="muted">{move || format!("{} legal moves", legal_count())}</p>
104	            </Show>
105	            <div class="sidebar-actions">
106	                <button class="btn" on:click=move |_| on_undo.call(()) disabled=move || wip>"Undo"</button>
107	                <button class="btn" on:click=move |_| on_new_game.call(()) disabled=move || wip>"New game"</button>
108	            </div>
109	            <FxToggles fx_confetti=fx_confetti fx_check_banner=fx_check_banner/>
110	            <a class="back-link" href=app_href("/") rel="external">"← Back to picker"</a>
111	        </aside>
112	    }
113	}
114	
115	#[component]
116	fn FxToggles(fx_confetti: RwSignal<bool>, fx_check_banner: RwSignal<bool>) -> impl IntoView {
117	    view! {
118	        <div class="fx-toggles">
119	            <label>
120	                <input
121	                    type="checkbox"
122	                    prop:checked=move || fx_confetti.get()
123	                    on:change=move |ev| fx_confetti.set(event_target_checked(&ev))
124	                />
125	                <span>"Victory effects (confetti + banner)"</span>
126	            </label>
127	            <label>
128	                <input
129	                    type="checkbox"
130	                    prop:checked=move || fx_check_banner.get()
131	                    on:change=move |ev| fx_check_banner.set(event_target_checked(&ev))
132	                />
133	                <span>"將軍 / CHECK warning"</span>
134	            </label>
135	        </div>
136	    }
137	}
138	
139	fn win_reason_label(reason: WinReason) -> &'static str {
140	    match reason {
141	        WinReason::Checkmate => "checkmate 將死",
142	        WinReason::Stalemate => "stalemate 困死",
143	        WinReason::Resignation => "resignation",
144	        WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces => "only one side has pieces",
145	        WinReason::Timeout => "timeout",
146	        WinReason::GeneralCaptured => "general captured",
147	    }
148	}
149	
150	fn draw_reason_label(reason: DrawReason) -> &'static str {
151	    match reason {
152	        DrawReason::NoProgress => "no progress",
153	        DrawReason::Repetition => "repetition",
154	        DrawReason::Agreed => "agreed",
155	        DrawReason::InsufficientMaterial => "insufficient material",
156	    }
157	}
```

```text
1	//! Pure-logic helpers for the client's view of a game. Native-testable —
2	//! no Leptos signals or browser deps live here.
3	
4	use chess_core::coord::Square;
5	use chess_core::moves::Move;
6	use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, Side};
7	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
8	
9	/// Role assigned by the server on `Hello` (player) or `Spectating`
10	/// (read-only). Drives whether the play page renders move/resign/rematch
11	/// affordances and whether the chat input is enabled.
12	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
13	pub enum ClientRole {
14	    Player(Side),
15	    Spectator,
16	}
17	
18	impl ClientRole {
19	    pub fn is_player(self) -> bool {
20	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Player(_))
21	    }
22	
23	    pub fn is_spectator(self) -> bool {
24	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Spectator)
25	    }
26	
27	    pub fn observer(self) -> Side {
28	        // Spectators render from RED's POV — matches what chess-net's
29	        // `broadcast_update` projects for spectator updates.
30	        match self {
31	            ClientRole::Player(s) => s,
32	            ClientRole::Spectator => Side::RED,
33	        }
34	    }
35	}
36	
37	/// Trim a vec from the front so it holds at most `max` entries. Used for
38	/// the per-page chat ring buffer that mirrors the server's 50-line cap.
39	pub fn truncate_front<T>(buf: &mut Vec<T>, max: usize) {
40	    if buf.len() > max {
41	        let drop_count = buf.len() - max;
42	        buf.drain(0..drop_count);
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	/// Find the legal `Move` (if any) whose origin is `from` and whose final
47	/// destination is `to`. Reveal moves match when `from == to == at`.
48	pub fn find_move(view: &PlayerView, from: Square, to: Square) -> Option<Move> {
49	    view.legal_moves.iter().find(|m| matches_endpoints(m, from, to)).cloned()
50	}
51	
52	fn matches_endpoints(mv: &Move, from: Square, to: Square) -> bool {
53	    if mv.origin_square() != from {
54	        return false;
55	    }
56	    match mv.to_square() {
57	        Some(t) => t == to,
58	        None => matches!(mv, Move::Reveal { at, .. } if *at == to),
59	    }
60	}
61	
62	/// All legal destination squares for a piece on `from` (used to render
63	/// move dots). In banqi 連吃 chain mode the engine adds
64	/// `Move::EndChain { at: chain_lock }` to `legal_moves`; that move has
65	/// `origin == chain_lock` and `to == None`, and is intentionally NOT a
66	/// movement target — it's the explicit "release the lock" gesture
67	/// triggered by clicking the locked piece. Excluding it here keeps
68	/// the dot/ring layer accurate during chain mode (and crucially keeps
69	/// this closure from panicking, which would freeze the leptos DOM
70	/// updates and leave stale markers on the board).
71	pub fn legal_targets(view: &PlayerView, from: Square) -> Vec<Square> {
72	    view.legal_moves
73	        .iter()
74	        .filter(|m| m.origin_square() == from)
75	        .filter_map(|m| match m {
76	            Move::Reveal { at, .. } => Some(*at),
77	            Move::EndChain { .. } => None,
78	            other => other.to_square(),
79	        })
80	        .collect()
81	}
82	
83	/// True if `view.chain_lock` is set and the click on `sq` should commit
84	/// `Move::EndChain` (e.g. user clicks the locked piece itself to release
85	/// the chain). Otherwise the click should attempt a further capture.
86	pub fn end_chain_move(view: &PlayerView) -> Option<Move> {
87	    view.chain_lock.map(|at| Move::EndChain { at })
88	}
89	
90	/// Sort order for the sidebar "captured pieces" panel.
91	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Default)]
92	pub enum CapturedSort {
93	    /// Chronological — order returned by `GameState::captured_pieces()`.
94	    #[default]
95	    Time,
96	    /// Largest piece first (General > Advisor > Elephant > Chariot >
97	    /// Horse > Cannon > Soldier).
98	    Rank,
99	}
100	
101	impl CapturedSort {
102	    pub fn toggled(self) -> Self {
103	        match self {
104	            CapturedSort::Time => CapturedSort::Rank,
105	            CapturedSort::Rank => CapturedSort::Time,
106	        }
107	    }
108	
109	    pub fn label(self) -> &'static str {
110	        match self {
111	            CapturedSort::Time => "time",
112	            CapturedSort::Rank => "rank",
113	        }
114	    }
115	}
116	
117	/// Rank value used by `CapturedSort::Rank`. Higher = stronger piece.
118	pub fn piece_rank_value(kind: PieceKind) -> u8 {
119	    match kind {
120	        PieceKind::General => 6,
121	        PieceKind::Advisor => 5,
122	        PieceKind::Elephant => 4,
123	        PieceKind::Chariot => 3,
124	        PieceKind::Horse => 2,
125	        PieceKind::Cannon => 1,
126	        PieceKind::Soldier => 0,
127	    }
128	}
129	
130	/// Split `pieces` (chronological from the engine) into per-side rows
131	/// for the sidebar panel, sorting each row according to `sort`.
132	pub fn split_and_sort_captured(pieces: &[Piece], sort: CapturedSort) -> (Vec<Piece>, Vec<Piece>) {
133	    let mut red: Vec<Piece> = pieces.iter().filter(|p| p.side == Side::RED).copied().collect();
134	    let mut black: Vec<Piece> = pieces.iter().filter(|p| p.side == Side::BLACK).copied().collect();
135	    if sort == CapturedSort::Rank {
136	        red.sort_by_key(|p| std::cmp::Reverse(piece_rank_value(p.kind)));
137	        black.sort_by_key(|p| std::cmp::Reverse(piece_rank_value(p.kind)));
138	    }
139	    (red, black)
140	}
141	
142	#[cfg(test)]
143	mod tests {
144	    use super::*;
145	    use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
146	    use chess_core::state::GameState;
147	
148	    #[test]
149	    fn find_move_locates_xiangqi_step() {
150	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
151	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
152	        // Red soldier at file 0 rank 3 should have a step forward to rank 4.
153	        let from = Square(3 * 9); // rank 3, file 0
154	        let to = Square(4 * 9); // rank 4, file 0
155	        let mv = find_move(&view, from, to);
156	        assert!(matches!(mv, Some(Move::Step { .. })), "expected step from soldier, got {:?}", mv);
157	    }
158	
159	    #[test]
160	    fn legal_targets_for_chariot_in_corner() {
161	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
162	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
163	        // Red chariot at (file 0, rank 0) — Square(0). Should have several
164	        // legal destinations along its file (rank moves blocked by horse).
165	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, Square(0));
166	        assert!(!targets.is_empty(), "chariot at corner must have legal moves");
167	    }
168	
169	    #[test]
170	    fn legal_targets_empty_for_non_piece_square() {
171	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
172	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
173	        // Square(5*9 + 4) — a river-ish empty square. Empty piece → no targets.
174	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, Square(5 * 9 + 4));
175	        assert!(targets.is_empty());
176	    }
177	
178	    #[test]
179	    fn truncate_front_drops_oldest_when_over_cap() {
180	        let mut v = vec![1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
181	        truncate_front(&mut v, 3);
182	        assert_eq!(v, vec![3, 4, 5]);
183	    }
184	
185	    #[test]
186	    fn truncate_front_noop_when_under_cap() {
187	        let mut v = vec![1, 2];
188	        truncate_front(&mut v, 5);
189	        assert_eq!(v, vec![1, 2]);
190	    }
191	
192	    #[test]
193	    fn client_role_observer_defaults_red_for_spectator() {
194	        assert_eq!(ClientRole::Player(Side::BLACK).observer(), Side::BLACK);
195	        assert_eq!(ClientRole::Spectator.observer(), Side::RED);
196	        assert!(ClientRole::Spectator.is_spectator());
197	        assert!(ClientRole::Player(Side::RED).is_player());
198	    }
199	
200	    #[test]
201	    fn end_chain_move_returns_some_when_chain_lock_set() {
202	        use chess_core::board::Board;
203	        use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};

237	        assert_eq!(view.chain_lock, Some(s1));
238	        assert!(matches!(end_chain_move(&view), Some(Move::EndChain { at }) if at == s1));
239	    }

241	    #[test]
242	    fn legal_targets_in_chain_mode_excludes_end_chain_and_does_not_panic() {
243	        // Regression for the "stale dots after chain capture" bug:
244	        // `legal_targets` was hitting an `unreachable!()` arm for
245	        // `Move::EndChain { at }` (whose `to_square()` returns None
246	        // and which doesn't match the `Reveal` arm). The panic froze
247	        // leptos DOM updates, leaving the previous chain step's
248	        // markers on screen. Now EndChain is filtered out here and
249	        // the closure returns a clean target list.
250	        use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
251	        use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare};
252	        use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
253	        use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
254	        use smallvec::smallvec;
255	
256	        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
257	        let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
258	        for sq in squares {
259	            state.board.set(sq, None);
260	        }
261	        state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
262	        let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
263	        let s1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
264	        let s2 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(3));
265	        state.board.set(h, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse))));
266	        state
267	            .board
268	            .set(s1, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier))));
269	        state
270	            .board
271	            .set(s2, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier))));
272	        // Capture s1, landing on s1 with chain mode active.
273	        let cap = Move::Capture {
274	            from: h,
275	            to: s1,
276	            captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
277	        };
278	        state.make_move(&cap).unwrap();
279	        assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, Some(s1));
280	
281	        let view = chess_core::view::PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
282	        // Confirm the engine actually emitted EndChain alongside the next-hop capture.
283	        assert!(view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| matches!(m, Move::EndChain { .. })));
284	
285	        // legal_targets must not panic and must not list the locked
286	        // square (where EndChain "lives") as a movement target.
287	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, s1);
288	        assert!(targets.contains(&s2), "next hop s2 must be a legal target");
289	        assert!(!targets.contains(&s1), "EndChain's `at` must NOT show up as a target dot");
290	    }

292	    #[test]

294	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
295	        let view = chess_core::view::PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
296	        assert!(end_chain_move(&view).is_none());
```

```rs
//! `chess-ai` — clean-room alpha-beta search for xiangqi.
//!
//! MVP per `.claude/plans/ai-vs-chatgpt-deep-research-docs-ai-dee-typed-stonebraker.md`:
//! negamax + alpha-beta, handcrafted material eval, capture-first move ordering,
//! seeded RNG for difficulty randomness. WASM-clean (no platform deps beyond
//! what `chess-core` already pulls in).
//!
//! Banqi is out of scope — `Move::DarkCapture` / `Move::Reveal` resolution in
//! `chess-core` is deterministic, which would let a search peek at hidden
//! tiles. Use this engine only with `Variant::Xiangqi`.

use chess_core::coord::Square;
use chess_core::moves::Move;
use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, Side};
use chess_core::state::GameState;
use rand::seq::SliceRandom;
use rand::{Rng, SeedableRng};
use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng;

#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
pub enum Difficulty {
    Easy,
    Normal,
    Hard,
}

impl Difficulty {
    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Difficulty::Easy => "easy",
            Difficulty::Normal => "normal",
            Difficulty::Hard => "hard",
        }
    }

    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {
        match s.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
            "easy" => Some(Difficulty::Easy),
            "normal" | "medium" => Some(Difficulty::Normal),
            "hard" => Some(Difficulty::Hard),
            _ => None,
        }
    }

    /// Default search depth for this difficulty.
    pub fn default_depth(self) -> u8 {
        match self {
            Difficulty::Easy => 1,
            Difficulty::Normal => 3,
            Difficulty::Hard => 4,
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct AiOptions {
    pub difficulty: Difficulty,
    /// Override `Difficulty::default_depth`. `None` uses the default.
    pub max_depth: Option<u8>,
    /// Optional seed for difficulty randomness (Easy top-N pick / Normal
    /// tiebreak). `None` falls back to a fixed seed so behaviour is
    /// deterministic — the caller may pass a fresh seed per move for variety.
    pub seed: Option<u64>,
}

impl AiOptions {
    pub fn new(difficulty: Difficulty) -> Self {
        Self { difficulty, max_depth: None, seed: None }
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct AiMoveResult {
    pub mv: Move,
    pub score: i32,
    pub depth: u8,
    pub nodes: u32,
}

/// Mate score — large enough that no material configuration can match it.
const MATE: i32 = 1_000_000;
/// Cap nodes-per-search to keep web UI snappy. Hit at depth 4 in busy
/// midgames; the engine returns the best-found-so-far when exceeded.
const NODE_BUDGET: u32 = 250_000;

/// Pick a move for the side to move in `state`.
///
/// Returns `None` only when there are no legal moves (caller should treat as
/// game over). Game-over states (`status != Ongoing`) also return `None`.
pub fn choose_move(state: &GameState, opts: &AiOptions) -> Option<AiMoveResult> {
    if !matches!(state.status, chess_core::state::GameStatus::Ongoing) {
        return None;
    }
    let moves = state.legal_moves();
    if moves.is_empty() {
        return None;
    }

    let depth = opts.max_depth.unwrap_or_else(|| opts.difficulty.default_depth()).max(1);
    let seed = opts.seed.unwrap_or(0xC0FFEE_u64);
    let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(seed);

    // Order root moves: captures first.
    let mut ordered: Vec<Move> = moves.into_iter().collect();
    ordered.sort_by_key(|m| if is_capture(m) { 0 } else { 1 });

    let mut work = state.clone();
    let mut nodes: u32 = 0;
    let mut scored: Vec<(Move, i32)> = Vec::with_capacity(ordered.len());
    let mut alpha = -MATE - 1;
    let beta = MATE + 1;

    for mv in ordered {
        if work.make_move(&mv).is_err() {
            continue;
        }
        let score = -negamax(&mut work, depth - 1, -beta, -alpha, &mut nodes);
        let _ = work.unmake_move();
        scored.push((mv, score));
        if score > alpha {
            alpha = score;
        }
        if nodes >= NODE_BUDGET {
            break;
        }
    }
    if scored.is_empty() {
        return None;
    }

    let best_score = scored.iter().map(|(_, s)| *s).max().unwrap();
    let chosen = match opts.difficulty {
        Difficulty::Easy => {
            // Pick uniformly from top-3 by score.
            let mut sorted: Vec<&(Move, i32)> = scored.iter().collect();
            sorted.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.cmp(&a.1));
            let take = sorted.len().min(3);
            sorted[..take].choose(&mut rng).map(|(m, s)| (m.clone(), *s)).unwrap()
        }
        Difficulty::Normal => {
            // Best-or-near-best: any move within 10cp of best, picked at random.
            let near: Vec<&(Move, i32)> =
                scored.iter().filter(|(_, s)| best_score - *s <= 10).collect();
            let pick = near.choose(&mut rng).copied().unwrap();
            (pick.0.clone(), pick.1)
        }
        Difficulty::Hard => {
            // Strict best (first occurrence on ties — deterministic).
            scored.iter().find(|(_, s)| *s == best_score).cloned().unwrap()
        }
    };

    // Tiny noise on Easy/Normal via the same rng, so identical positions
    // with different seeds vary even when scores tie cleanly. (Hard stays
    // stable.)
    let _: u8 = rng.gen();

    Some(AiMoveResult { mv: chosen.0, score: chosen.1, depth, nodes })
}

fn negamax(state: &mut GameState, depth: u8, mut alpha: i32, beta: i32, nodes: &mut u32) -> i32 {
    *nodes = nodes.saturating_add(1);
    let moves = state.legal_moves();
    if moves.is_empty() {
        // No legal moves under Asian rules → loss for the side to move
        // (checkmate or stalemate). Returning a depth-relative mate score
        // makes the search prefer faster mates and slower losses.
        return -MATE + depth as i32;
    }
    if depth == 0 || *nodes >= NODE_BUDGET {
        return evaluate(state);
    }

    // Capture-first ordering. Cheap — single pass over the SmallVec.
    let mut ordered: Vec<Move> = moves.into_iter().collect();
    ordered.sort_by_key(|m| if is_capture(m) { 0 } else { 1 });

    let mut best = -MATE - 1;
    for mv in ordered {
        if state.make_move(&mv).is_err() {
            continue;
        }
        let score = -negamax(state, depth - 1, -beta, -alpha, nodes);
        let _ = state.unmake_move();
        if score > best {
            best = score;
        }
        if score > alpha {
            alpha = score;
        }
        if alpha >= beta {
            break;
        }
    }
    best
}

#[inline]
fn is_capture(m: &Move) -> bool {
    matches!(
        m,
        Move::Capture { .. }
            | Move::CannonJump { .. }
            | Move::ChainCapture { .. }
            | Move::DarkCapture { .. }
    )
}

/// Side-relative material eval for xiangqi. Positive favours the side to
/// move. Banqi will get its own evaluator when (if) it gets an engine —
/// see ADR / TODO.
fn evaluate(state: &GameState) -> i32 {
    let me = state.side_to_move;
    let mut score = 0i32;
    for sq in state.board.squares() {
        let Some(pos) = state.board.get(sq) else { continue };
        if !pos.revealed {
            continue;
        }
        let v = piece_value(state, pos.piece, sq);
        if pos.piece.side == me {
            score += v;
        } else {
            score -= v;
        }
    }
    score
}

fn piece_value(state: &GameState, p: Piece, sq: Square) -> i32 {
    match p.kind {
        // General is excluded from material — checkmate is handled by the
        // mate score. Casual xiangqi (where a missing general is the loss
        // condition) still works because `legal_moves` going empty after
        // capture is the recursion's terminal.
        PieceKind::General => 0,
        PieceKind::Advisor => 200,
        PieceKind::Elephant => 200,
        PieceKind::Chariot => 900,
        PieceKind::Horse => 400,
        PieceKind::Cannon => 450,
        PieceKind::Soldier => {
            let (_, rank) = state.board.file_rank(sq);
            // Xiangqi river: ranks 0-4 are Red half, 5-9 are Black half.
            let crossed = match p.side {
                Side::RED => rank.0 >= 5,
                _ => rank.0 <= 4,
            };
            if crossed {
                200
            } else {
                100
            }
        }
    }
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;

    #[test]
    fn opening_xiangqi_returns_a_legal_move_each_difficulty() {
        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
        for diff in [Difficulty::Easy, Difficulty::Normal, Difficulty::Hard] {
            let opts = AiOptions { difficulty: diff, max_depth: Some(2), seed: Some(7) };
            let result = choose_move(&state, &opts).expect("must return a move");
            // The returned move must be in the legal-move list.
            let legal = state.legal_moves();
            assert!(
                legal.iter().any(|m| m == &result.mv),
                "{:?} returned non-legal move {:?}",
                diff,
                result.mv
            );
        }
    }

    #[test]
    fn no_legal_moves_returns_none() {
        // Synthesise: clear the board entirely, set status by manipulating.
        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
        let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
        for sq in squares {
            state.board.set(sq, None);
        }
        // legal_moves() should now be empty.
        assert!(state.legal_moves().is_empty());
        let opts = AiOptions::new(Difficulty::Hard);
        assert!(choose_move(&state, &opts).is_none());
    }

    #[test]
    fn determinism_same_seed_same_move() {
        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
        let opts = AiOptions { difficulty: Difficulty::Easy, max_depth: Some(2), seed: Some(42) };
        let a = choose_move(&state, &opts).unwrap();
        let b = choose_move(&state, &opts).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(a.mv, b.mv);
    }

    #[test]
    fn hard_prefers_capture_when_free() {
        // Build a position where Red has a free capture: place a Red
        // chariot opposite a Black soldier with nothing in between. A
        // material-aware engine at any depth ≥ 1 must take.
        use chess_core::board::Board;
        use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank};
        use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare};

        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
        // Wipe and seed.
        let board: Board = state.board.clone();
        let squares: Vec<Square> = board.squares().collect();
        for sq in squares {
            state.board.set(sq, None);
        }
        // Generals so legal_moves stays valid; place far apart.
        let red_gen = state.board.sq(File(4), Rank(0));
        let blk_gen = state.board.sq(File(4), Rank(9));
        state
            .board
            .set(red_gen, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::General))));
        state.board.set(
            blk_gen,
            Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::General))),
        );
        // Red chariot at (0,4); Black soldier at (0,5). Chariot goes north
        // one step to capture for free.
        let red_chariot = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(4));
        let blk_soldier = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(5));
        state.board.set(
            red_chariot,
            Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot))),
        );
        state.board.set(
            blk_soldier,
            Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier))),
        );

        let opts = AiOptions { difficulty: Difficulty::Hard, max_depth: Some(2), seed: Some(0) };
        let result = choose_move(&state, &opts).unwrap();
        match result.mv {
            Move::Capture { from, to, .. } => {
                assert_eq!(from, red_chariot);
                assert_eq!(to, blk_soldier);
            }
            other => panic!("expected capture, got {:?}", other),
        }
    }
}
```

_**User (2026-05-08 14:45:15Z)**_

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user asked to implement a "player vs AI" (single-player vs computer) feature for their Rust+WASM Chinese chess (xiangqi/banqi) web app. They referenced an existing deep research report at `docs/ai-deep-research-report.md` and provided a detailed multi-phase prompt strategy in Chinese, emphasizing: (a) MVP should be a clean-room lightweight alpha-beta engine (no RL, no Pikafish, no LLM, no Web Worker initially); (b) reuse existing legal-move pipeline; (c) minimal-invasive integration; (d) support easy/normal/hard difficulty with depth/randomness; (e) UI mode toggle, side selection, AI thinking indicator. The user wanted the agent to first plan, then implement.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Rust workspace with Cargo, multiple crates (chess-core, chess-ai, chess-net, chess-engine, chess-tui, chess-web)
   - Leptos 0.6 (CSR mode) + Trunk + WASM browser client
   - Negamax + alpha-beta pruning search
   - Material evaluation, capture-first move ordering
   - `wasm_bindgen_futures::spawn_local` + `gloo-timers::future::TimeoutFuture` for async AI tasks
   - Epoch-based cancellation pattern for stale async tasks
   - `Move::Reveal { revealed: Option<Piece> }` ABI boundary (banqi excluded from AI MVP)
   - `rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng` for deterministic seeded RNG (WASM-safe)
   - URL query-string encoding of mode/ai_side/ai_difficulty params
   - Plan-mode workflow with `.claude/plans/<name>.md`
   - target-gated `cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")` deps in chess-web

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - `.claude/plans/ai-vs-chatgpt-deep-research-docs-ai-dee-typed-stonebraker.md` (created)
     - Plan file specifying alpha-beta MVP for xiangqi only, banqi deferred due to hidden-info issue.

   - `crates/chess-ai/Cargo.toml` (rewritten)
     ```toml
     [package]
     name        = "chess-ai"
     description = "Lightweight alpha-beta search engine for xiangqi. MVP — see plan."
     # ... workspace inheritance ...
     [dependencies]
     chess-core  = { path = "../chess-core" }
     rand        = { workspace = true }
     rand_chacha = { workspace = true }
     ```

   - `crates/chess-ai/src/lib.rs` (full implementation, ~250 lines)
     - `Difficulty` enum with `as_str`, `from_str`, `default_depth` (Easy=1, Normal=3, Hard=4)
     - `AiOptions { difficulty, max_depth, seed }`
     - `AiMoveResult { mv, score, depth, nodes }`
     - `choose_move(state: &GameState, opts: &AiOptions) -> Option<AiMoveResult>` — clones state, runs root search, applies difficulty selection
     - `negamax(state: &mut GameState, depth, alpha, beta, nodes) -> i32`
     - `evaluate(state)` returns side-to-move-relative material score
     - `piece_value` table: General 0, Advisor 200, Elephant 200, Chariot 900, Horse 400, Cannon 450, Soldier 100/200 past river
     - MATE = 1_000_000, NODE_BUDGET = 250_000
     - 4 unit tests (all pass): opening returns legal move per difficulty, no-legal-moves returns None, determinism with same seed, hard prefers free capture

   - `clients/chess-web/Cargo.toml` (edited)
     - Added `chess-ai = { path = "../../crates/chess-ai" }` to base deps
     - Added `gloo-timers = { version = "0.3", features = ["futures"] }` to wasm32 target deps

   - `clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs` (edited)
     - Added `PlayMode` enum (Pvp default, VsAi)
     - Extended `LocalRulesParams` struct with `mode: PlayMode`, `ai_side: Side`, `ai_difficulty: Difficulty`
     - Manual `Default` impl (default ai_side = Black, ai_difficulty = Normal)
     - `parse_local_rules` reads `mode=ai|vsai`, `ai=red|black`, `diff=easy|normal|hard`
     - `build_local_query` emits these for xiangqi when mode is VsAi
     - Added 2 new tests: `xiangqi_vs_ai_query_round_trips`, `xiangqi_default_mode_omits_ai_query`
     - Fixed test `build_local_href_includes_query_when_non_default` to use `..Default::default()`

   - `clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs` (edited)
     - Added imports for `chess_ai::Difficulty`, `Side`, `PlayMode`
     - XiangqiCard now has 4 reactive signals: strict, mode, player_red, difficulty
     - Mode radio (Pass-and-play / vs Computer)
     - `<Show when=...VsAi>` reveals "You play" (Red/Black) and "Difficulty" (Easy/Normal/Hard) fieldsets
     - BanqiCard fixed to `LocalRulesParams { house: house(), seed, ..Default::default() }`

   - `clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs` (edited)
     - Added imports for `AiOptions`, `Difficulty`, `GameStatus`, `PlayMode`
     - Added `VsAiConfig { ai_side, difficulty }` struct
     - `LocalPage` resolves `ai: Option<VsAiConfig>` (only Some when xiangqi + VsAi)
     - `LocalGame` takes `ai: Option<VsAiConfig>` param
     - Added signals: `ai_thinking: RwSignal<bool>`, `move_epoch: RwSignal<u32>`
     - `observer = ai.map(|c| c.ai_side.opposite()).unwrap_or(Side::RED)` so player's side is on bottom
     - `player_can_act = Signal::derive(...)` gates clicks during AI turn / thinking / wip
     - All `state.update` sites in click/end_chain handlers now bump `move_epoch`
     - `on_undo`: pops 2 plies in vs-AI mode (so player gets back to their turn)
     - `on_new_game`: bumps epoch, clears ai_thinking
     - AI move pump: `create_effect` watching view's side_to_move; when AI's turn → `spawn_local(async move { gloo_timers::future::TimeoutFuture::new(80).await; choose_move; epoch check; state.update })`
     - Added `<Show when=ai_thinking>` banner with text "⏳ AI thinking…"

4. Errors and fixes:
   - First `replace_all=true` Edit on local.rs failed with "String to replace not found" because the chain-mode block had different surrounding text than expected. Fixed by reading the file region and using a more precise non-replace_all edit for the chain end-chain + chain capture sites.
   - `wasm32-unknown-unknown` target not installed initially — fixed by running `RUSTUP_DIST_SERVER=https://static.rust-lang.org rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown` (per CLAUDE.md tuna-mirror workaround).
   - `cargo fmt --check` reported formatting diffs in 3 files — ran `cargo fmt` to auto-fix.
   - `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets` was attempted but failed mid-download due to network timeouts on `tokio v1.52.2` and `brotli v8.0.2` from crates.io. Not a code error — environment/network issue.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Banqi exclusion: identified that `chess-core::make_move` deterministically resolves `Move::Reveal { revealed: None }` to `Some(piece)` server-side, which would let a search peek at hidden tiles → minimax cheats. Solution: gate vs-AI to xiangqi only; picker shows toggle on Xiangqi card only; local page falls back to PvP if mode=ai on banqi/three-kingdom.
   - Stale AI move after undo: solved with `move_epoch` counter incremented on every state mutation; AI task captures epoch at start and at each await point, discards result on mismatch.
   - Infinite-AI-replay on undo: in vs-AI mode, undo pops 2 plies so player is back on move (otherwise undoing AI's reply leaves AI to move again → effect re-fires).
   - "AI thinking…" UI feedback: 80ms `TimeoutFuture` yield before search lets the banner paint before the synchronous search blocks the main thread.

6. All user messages:
   - Initial Chinese-language message: "本次實現的關鍵是要實作「玩家可以跟AI玩」的部分（單人vs電腦）這是ChatGPT做的Deep Research @docs/ai-deep-research-report.md 然後下面是它設計的Prompt 你可以參考著來" (followed by extensive prompt template) — request to implement vs-AI mode following the research's MVP-first guidance.
   - "Tool loaded." (after ToolSearch loads ExitPlanMode/TaskCreate/TaskUpdate schemas — system message, not real user input)
   - System notification of plan approval (no additional text from user beyond approving the plan).

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Task #4: Verify build + tests + WASM
     - Re-run `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` (interrupted by network timeout)

ADR-0006 captures the spectator opt-in choice (`?role=spectator` rather than auto-fallback) for v2 back-compat. 8 new server-smoke tests + 5 new round-trip tests; v2 RoomSummary still decodes via `serde(default)`.
```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(chess-ai,chess-web): local vs-AI mode (alpha-beta MVP, xiangqi only)

Ships a clean-room negamax + alpha-beta engine in `chess-ai` and wires it
into the chess-web Local page as an opt-in vs-Computer mode on the
Xiangqi card. Engine + UI both run entirely in WASM — no server, no
worker — so this works on the static GitHub Pages deploy.

- chess-ai: `choose_move(state, opts)` with negamax + capture-first
  ordering, handcrafted material eval (Chariot 900 / Cannon 450 / Horse
  400 / A+E 200 / Soldier 100/200 past river), Easy/Normal/Hard at
  depth 1/3/4, seeded `ChaCha8Rng` for difficulty randomness. 250k
  node budget cap.
- routes: `LocalRulesParams` gains `mode`/`ai_side`/`ai_difficulty`;
  URL tokens `?mode=ai&ai=red|black&diff=easy|normal|hard`. Default
  pvp keeps existing URLs byte-identical.
- picker: Xiangqi card surfaces mode + side + difficulty radios.
  Banqi/three-kingdom intentionally hide the toggle (banqi reveal
  resolution would let minimax peek at hidden tiles).
- local page: epoch-cancelled AI move pump via `spawn_local`, board
  click-locked while AI is on move, "AI thinking…" banner, undo pops
  two plies in vs-AI so the player gets back on move.

Banqi/ISMCTS, iterative deepening, Zobrist TT, quiescence, Web Worker
off-main-thread, and chess-tui vs-AI all remain on the existing
`backlog/chess-ai-search.md` follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```
