All green. Both `ci` (fmt + clippy + test + wasm-cleanliness) and `pages` (build + deploy) succeeded.

## Summary

**Backlog status**: existing entry is `TODO.md:58` → `backlog/chess-ai-search.md` ("alpha-beta + iterative deepening + Zobrist transposition for xiangqi; banqi needs ISMCTS"). The MVP we just shipped is Phase 1 of that — the entry stays open for the larger items (TT, iterative deepening, quiescence, ISMCTS for banqi). The backlog .md itself is still a template (never filled in) — fine to leave for now or flesh out when Phase 2 starts.

**WASM on GitHub Pages**: yes, the AI runs natively in the browser. `chess-ai` only adds `rand` + `rand_chacha` (already WASM-clean — banqi shuffle uses them). No worker, no server. The `pages` workflow built and deployed both commits successfully. Vs-AI is live now at the Pages URL — only the online lobby is unavailable on the static deploy (needs chess-net).

**CI**: clippy 1.95 caught a `sort_by_key` lint that 1.93 didn't (recurrence #4 of the version drift documented in `backlog/rust-toolchain-pin.md`); fixed in a follow-up commit. Both `ci` and `pages` are green on `b0be37c`.

## Next steps

1. **Validate on the live Pages deploy** — open `/local/xiangqi?mode=ai&ai=red&diff=hard`, play a full game, confirm AI reply latency on mobile (Hard depth 4 may be ~1–2s on phones; if painful, drop default depth or add a Web Worker — tracked in `backlog/chess-ai-search.md`).
2. **Banqi vs-AI** — needs determinization / ISMCTS (Phase 2; today's engine would cheat by seeing future reveals). Add a stub entry to `backlog/chess-ai-search.md` or split it out.
3. **chess-tui vs-AI** — engine API is reusable from native; small TUI wiring task. Add to `TODO.md`.
4. **Phase-2 search work** — iterative deepening, Zobrist TT, quiescence, MVV-LVA ordering, Web Worker off-main-thread. All belong in the existing AI backlog entry.
5. **Pin rust-toolchain** — recurrence #4 of clippy version drift today; `backlog/rust-toolchain-pin.md` says trigger is "third recurrence". We're past that — worth pinning soon.

68	fn LocalGame(variant: Variant, rules: RuleSet, wip: bool, ai: Option<VsAiConfig>) -> impl IntoView {

76	    // moves landing after the player undid or restarted.
77	    let move_epoch = create_rw_signal::<u32>(0);

79	    // Render from the human player's POV when in vs-AI mode so the player's

92	    // next one. Also a safety net for any future click path that
93	    // forgets its own `selected.set(None)`.
94	    let side_signal = Signal::derive(move || view.with(|v| v.side_to_move));

107	
108	    let effective_selected: Signal<Option<Square>> = Signal::derive(move || {
109	        // In chain mode the engine dictates which piece moves; surface

111	        // render around it.
112	        chain_locked_signal.get().or_else(|| selected.get())
113	    });
114	
115	    // Whether the human can act right now. In vs-AI mode the human is

118	        if wip {
119	            return false;
120	        }

122	            None => true,
123	            Some(cfg) => {

127	                view.with(|v| v.side_to_move != cfg.ai_side)
128	            }
129	        }
130	    });
131	
132	    let on_end_chain: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_| {
133	        if !player_can_act.get_untracked() {
134	            return;
135	        }
136	        let v = view.get();
137	        if let Some(mv) = end_chain_move(&v) {
138	            state.update(|s| {
139	                s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal end-chain");
140	                s.refresh_status();
141	            });
142	            selected.set(None);
143	            move_epoch.update(|n| *n = n.wrapping_add(1));
144	        }
145	    });
146	
147	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
148	        if !player_can_act.get_untracked() {
149	            return;
150	        }
151	        let v = view.get();
152	
153	        // Chain mode is engine-driven: when chain_lock is set, only the
154	        // locked piece can move (captures only). Clicking the locked
155	        // piece itself is the explicit "end chain" gesture.
156	        if let Some(locked) = v.chain_lock {
157	            if sq == locked {

162	                    });
163	                    move_epoch.update(|n| *n = n.wrapping_add(1));
164	                }
165	                selected.set(None);
166	                return;
167	            }
168	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, locked, sq) {
169	                state.update(|s| {
170	                    s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal chain capture");
171	                    s.refresh_status();
172	                });
173	                selected.set(None);
174	                move_epoch.update(|n| *n = n.wrapping_add(1));
175	                return;
176	            }
177	            // Click somewhere else during chain mode: ignore (the user

179	            return;
180	        }
181	
182	        let cur = selected.get();
183	
184	        if cur == Some(sq) {

186	            return;
187	        }
188	
189	        if let Some(from) = cur {

196	                move_epoch.update(|n| *n = n.wrapping_add(1));
197	                return;
198	            }
199	        }
200	
201	        let cell = v.cells[sq.0 as usize];

205	        let is_selectable_revealed = matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Revealed(_))

207	        if is_selectable_revealed {
208	            selected.set(Some(sq));
209	        } else if matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Hidden) {
210	            // Banqi flip — engine emits `Reveal { at }` whose origin and

212	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, sq, sq) {
213	                state.update(|s| {
214	                    s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal reveal");
215	                    s.refresh_status();
216	                });
217	                selected.set(None);
218	                move_epoch.update(|n| *n = n.wrapping_add(1));
219	            }
220	        } else {
221	            selected.set(None);
222	        }
223	    });
224	
225	    let on_undo: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_| {
226	        if wip {
227	            return;
228	        }
229	        // In vs-AI mode, undo two plies so the player is back on move.
230	        // (Undoing only one would put the AI on the move and the AI effect

235	        let plies = match ai {

237	            None => 1,
238	        };
239	        state.update(|s| {

243	                }
244	            }

250	    });
251	
252	    let on_new_game: Callback<()> = Callback::new({
253	        let initial_rules = initial_rules.clone();

255	            state.set(GameState::new(initial_rules.clone()));
256	            selected.set(None);
257	            ai_thinking.set(false);
258	            move_epoch.update(|n| *n = n.wrapping_add(1));
259	        }
260	    });

262	    // AI move pump. Fires whenever the side-to-move flips to the AI in a

279	            ai_thinking.set(true);

312	    let prefs = expect_context::<Prefs>();

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-08 15:08:56Z)**_

Found the bug. The effect tracks `view.get()` AND `move_epoch.get()`, but the click handler does `state.update(...)` first, then `move_epoch.update(...)`. So:

1. Player clicks → `state.update` fires → view changes → effect runs **with old epoch** → `cur_epoch = N (old)`, spawns task.
2. `move_epoch.update` bumps to N+1 → effect runs again, but `ai_thinking == true` so early-returns.
3. Async task wakes → `move_epoch.get_untracked() == N+1 != cur_epoch (N)` → bails out, sets `ai_thinking = false`.
4. AI never moves.

This works on the *very first* AI=Red move because no epoch bump has happened yet (still 0 == 0). Every subsequent AI turn fails the epoch check.

Fix: track **only** `move_epoch` in the effect, read the view via `with_untracked`. Then the effect runs once per epoch bump, after both state and epoch are settled.

```md
# Leptos effect captures stale epoch when tracking both upstream + token

**Symptom**

Local vs-AI on `/local/xiangqi?mode=ai&...`: the AI made its very first move (when AI=Red goes first) but never moved again. AI=Black configurations never moved at all. No "AI thinking…" banner appeared on the player's stuck turn. Engine unit tests all passed; the move pump in `clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs` was the culprit.

**Setup**

The page has a `move_epoch: RwSignal<u32>` bumped on every state-mutating event (player click, AI move, undo, new game). The intent is "AI task captures epoch at start, drops the result if epoch changed by the time `choose_move` returns." Click handlers were ordered:

\```rust
state.update(|s| { s.make_move(&mv).unwrap(); s.refresh_status(); });
selected.set(None);
move_epoch.update(|n| *n = n.wrapping_add(1));
\```

The AI effect was:

\```rust
create_effect(move |_| {
    let cur_epoch = move_epoch.get();
    let v = view.get();                        // ← also tracked
    if v.side_to_move != cfg.ai_side { return; }
    if ai_thinking.get_untracked() { return; }
    ai_thinking.set(true);
    let snapshot = state.get_untracked();
    spawn_local(async move {
        TimeoutFuture::new(80).await;
        if move_epoch.get_untracked() != cur_epoch {
            ai_thinking.set(false);
            return;                             // ← always tripped
        }
        ...
    });
});
\```

**Root cause**

Leptos fires the effect on *every* tracked-signal change. Because `view.get()` was tracked, the effect re-ran twice per click:

1. `state.update(...)` mutates state → `view` memo recomputes → effect runs. `move_epoch` is **still old**, so `cur_epoch` captures the stale value. Sees AI's turn, sets `ai_thinking=true`, spawns the task with `cur_epoch = N`.
2. `move_epoch.update(...)` bumps to N+1 → effect runs again. `ai_thinking` is now `true` (set by run #1) → early-return.
3. Task wakes after 80 ms: `move_epoch.get_untracked() == N+1`, captured `cur_epoch == N` → mismatch → bails out, sets `ai_thinking=false`. No AI move.

The first AI=Red move worked because the effect runs once on mount with `move_epoch == 0` and no prior bump — `0 == 0` so the check passes.

**Fix**

Track only the token (`move_epoch`); read the upstream state via `with_untracked`. The effect then runs exactly once per epoch bump, after both `state` and `move_epoch` have settled:

\```rust
create_effect(move |_| {
    let cur_epoch = move_epoch.get();
    let v = view.get_untracked();              // ← no longer tracked
    if v.side_to_move != cfg.ai_side { return; }
    ...
});
\```

**Why this is a class of bug, not a one-off**

Any "epoch token + content signal" pattern in Leptos has the same trap when click handlers update them in the order `content → token`. Either:

- Track only the token (this fix), **or**
- Reorder click handlers so the token is bumped *first*, then content (works but forces every callsite to remember the order — fragile).

The tracked-content version is also wasteful: it runs the effect twice per click for no benefit.

**Test that would have caught it**

Hard to unit-test directly — the buggy code is `#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]`-gated and depends on Leptos's reactive runtime. Integration test options for follow-up:

- A `wasm-bindgen-test` covering "after N player moves, AI has made N AI moves" — would need the leptos-test runtime.
- A Playwright test against `make play-web` that drives clicks and asserts the AI replies (tracked in `backlog/web-playwright.md`).
- Extracting the move-pump decision into a pure helper `fn ai_should_move(view, cfg, ai_thinking) -> bool` — testable with `cargo test`, but doesn't catch the *timing* aspect (which is the actual bug).

**See also**

- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs` — fix landed here.
- `.claude/plans/ai-vs-chatgpt-deep-research-docs-ai-dee-typed-stonebraker.md` — original plan introducing the pump.

The vs-AI effect tracked both `move_epoch.get()` and `view.get()`. Click

Both green. Fix is deployed.

## Summary

**Bug**: classic Leptos signal-tracking gotcha. The AI effect tracked both `move_epoch` and `view`. Since click handlers do `state.update` *before* `move_epoch.update`, the effect fired with a stale epoch on the state-change tick, captured `cur_epoch = N (old)`, then the async task's post-search epoch check (`get_untracked == N+1 != N`) bailed out. The first AI=Red move escaped because `0 == 0`.

**Fix**: track only `move_epoch` in the effect; read `view` via `with_untracked`. Class-of-bug doc at `pitfalls/leptos-effect-tracking-stale-epoch.md`.

**About "AI 笨笨的隨機挑"**: that part is real but separate. The eval is **material-only** with no piece-square tables, so opening positions are nearly all tied at score 0 → difficulty randomness picks among them → looks random. Hard at depth 4 will start preferring lines once captures appear, but the opening 5–10 moves stay weak until PSTs land.

## Test coverage

The buggy code is `cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")` + Leptos reactive runtime — can't unit-test directly with plain `cargo test`. Three follow-ups noted in the pitfall doc:
- Extract a pure `fn ai_should_move(view, cfg, ai_thinking) -> bool` helper (catches logic, not timing).
- `wasm-bindgen-test` with leptos test runtime.
- Playwright E2E (already on backlog: `web-playwright.md`).

## Updated next steps

1. **Verify the live deploy**: hard-refresh the GitHub Pages site, play AI=Black + AI=Red, confirm AI replies on every turn.
2. **Add piece-square tables (PSTs)** to `chess-ai::evaluate` — fixes the "random opening" feel. Cheap win: ~20 LOC per piece kind, big perceived strength jump. Belongs in `backlog/chess-ai-search.md`.
3. **Promote `web-playwright.md`** so we have an E2E test asserting "AI replies within 2s after every player move" — would have caught this bug.
4. **Phase-2 search** (iterative deepening, Zobrist TT, quiescence, MVV-LVA, Web Worker) — same backlog entry.
5. **Banqi vs-AI** via ISMCTS — separate research item.
6. **chess-tui vs-AI** — small wiring task; engine is reusable.
7. **Pin rust-toolchain** — clippy version drift hit again today (recurrence #4).
